title: Software Developer
description:
**Vi letar nu efter en Software Developer till ett globalt företag i Huskvarna. Start är i juni, 12 månader tidsbegränsat uppdrag till att börja med.**

**Uppdragsbeskrivning**


Utveckling av PC-verktyg och produktionssupport.

*Positionen/rollen ingår i en liten grupp som har ansvar för att:*

* Utveckla interna verktyg för konfigurering, testning, felsökning för att underlätta arbetet för R&D mm.
* Ansvara för firmware-deployment. Det innebär planering av hur systemet ska uppdateras, hur firmware ska packas och hur man sen säkerställer att rätt paket hamnar i rätt produkt.
* Ansvara för kontakten med produktionsteknik och återförsäljarverktygsansvariga gällande programmering av produkter.

**Krav**

* Erfarenhet av följande: C#, WPF, .NET-framework, Python, CI/CD, Git, Power BI, Seriekommunikation.
* Kommunikativ.

*Meriterande*: AWS (IaC, Python-CDK), Javascript, Azure Devops, Django, Databricks, embedded software development, mätverktygtsintegraion (ex. PicoScope, Delta, EA, mm)

**Den här rollen kräver flytande kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.**  



Detta är ett konsultuppdrag på 100% via Incluso i Huskvarna. Start är i juni, 12 månader tidsbegränsat uppdrag till att börja med, möjlighet till förlängning efter det. Det här uppdraget är 100% on-site i Huskvarna.  

  

Vänligen sök med CV och motivering via länken nedan. Vi presenterar kandidater löpande.

  

Ansvarig rekryterare på Incluso är Kanika Singhal.

title: Mjukvaruutvecklare till UMS Skeldar i Linköping
description:
###### **UMS Skeldar, ett mångfacetterat företag, med förmågor inom forskning, utveckling, design, tillverkning, testning, utbildning, underhåll och mycket mer. Vi utvecklar en av världens mest avancerade Unmanned Aerial Vehicle. Bli en del av vårt växande team!**


Key word: *Software, Software Engineer, Mjukvaruutvecklare, Engineer, C++, .NET C#.*

###### **Nu söker vi en Software Design Engineer med programmeringskunskap inom C++ och/eller .NET C#.**


Du blir del av teamet System & Software som består ca 35 medarbetare i roller som systemingenjörer, mjukvaruutvecklare, HMI-designer och testare. Teamet jobbar krossfunktionellt och med alla tre delar i helikoptersystemet: Helikopter, Markstation och Simulatorn.

### **I rollen som Mjukvaruutvecklare hos oss:**

* Programmera i hela eller delar av systemet;


	+ I helikoptern sker utvecklingen i C++.
	+ Simulatorn, som används för att testköra funktionalitet med hjälp av simulerade helikopterkörningar, är utvecklad i C++.
	+ Markstationen, som används för planering och genomförande av uppdrag med systemet, är till större delen utvecklad i .NET C# och WPF.
* Utveckla nya funktioner som inkluderar design och implementation av mjukvara.
* Utveckla automatiska testfall.
* Arbeta med underhåll av befintlig mjukvara med buggrättning och förbättringsåtgärder.

**Låter tjänsten intressant? Tveka inte att söka redan idag!**

### **Är du den vi söker?**


Vi lägger stor vikt vid personliga egenskaper och för att du ska trivas så bra som möjligt! Vi söker en person som uppskattar, trivs och bidrar till ett nära och gott samarbete med sina kollegor. Samtidigt har du förmåga att driva dina uppgifter självgående och tar ansvar för uppgifterna samt dina och företagets mål.


För att lyckas i den här rollen ser vi att du har en högskole- eller universitetsutbildning inom mjukvaruutveckling eller liknande områden. Det är meriterande om du har arbetslivserfarenhet men inte ett krav. Du kan programmera i .NET C# och/eller C++. Kan du WPF är det meriterande med inte ett krav.


Tjänsten ställer krav på mycket goda kunskaper i såväl engelska som svenska i tal och skrift.

### **Vi erbjuder:**

* en möjlighet att vara en del av ett dynamiskt företag som ständigt utvecklas, där din påverkan spelar en viktig roll
* en högteknologisk produkt som just nu ligger i framkant på marknaden
* en familjär arbetskultur som karaktäriseras av samarbete och högt engagemang
* aktiviteter som främjar både social gemenskap och hälsa, genom vår Festkommitté, Hälsokommitté, Brädspelskommitté och ett Kvinnligt Nätverk.

### **Ytterligare information:**

* Arbetsplats: Linköping.
* Tjänstgöringsgrad: Heltid.
* Start: Omgående med hänsyn till uppsägningstid.


För en anställning hos oss på UMS Skeldar krävs att du genomgår och godkänns enligt vid var tid gällande bestämmelser för säkerhetsskydd. Roller där vi har krav på säkerhetsklassinplacering kan, i förekommande fall, medföra krav på visst medborgarskap.

 **Vi behandlar ansökningar löpande. Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!**

title: iOS Developer
description:
We now have a new requirement and are looking for a consultant colleague with the qualifications listed below. The position is part of our consulting business, which means you will be employed by us and work either with clients or on internal projects and assignments.

*As part of your application, you may be presented to clients/partners as part of the recruitment process. If selected for the assignment, you will be employed by us and work either with clients or on internal projects and assignments as part of our consulting business.*

 **Your Profile (Must-have requirements)**  

* Excellent knowledge of app development with a focus on iOS, Swift concurrency, and SwiftUI
* Great collaboration skills and be a good listener
* Good knowledge of design patterns and best practices for iOS
* Active use of AI as support in coding and product development
* Several years of experience in systems development
* Experience working in teams with agile processes
* A willingness to develop and share experiences

  

**Application deadline:** 08-04-2026 **About Us**  

We currently employ experienced consultants in areas such as System Development, AI, Machine Learning, Testing, Cloud, Infra DevOps, and IT Project Management.  

  

Working at Deploja is something entirely different from working at an ordinary consulting company—and we are really proud of that. So, what makes us unique?  

  

We simply offer more than just a high salary and greater freedom; that’s just the beginning. With us, you’ll find the work environment and opportunities you deserve. Welcome to Deploja, where the grass is indeed greener!

 **Vi erbjuder dig följande, Valet är ditt!**


Exciting assignments with our many clients. We focus on long-term projects, which gives you the opportunity to create value for the client while also developing yourself.


A secure monthly salary – together we’ll agree on a base salary depending on your assignments.


Company car – we encourage you to choose an electric car for a more sustainable future.


Occupational pension and great opportunities for salary conversion.


Vacation – Would you like more than 30 days of vacation, perhaps 60 days?


Private health insurance, accident insurance, and life insurance for increased security.


Skills development – you set your own budget and choose the training you wish to attend.


Quality of life – Are you struggling to balance everyday life and wish to work a little less while still earning as much, if not more?

*We handle applications continuously, and due to the high workload, it's especially important to include a short motivation for the position, explaining how you meet the requirements.*

title: Sales Developer till Victoriahem
description:
Är du analytisk och gillar att förbättra samt utveckla processer med affären och kundnöjdhet i fokus? Då kan det här vara rollen för dig!


Nu rekryterar vi en Sales Developer som vill vara med och utveckla vårt sälj- och uthyrningsarbete genom data, analys och smartare arbetssätt. Det här är en perfekt roll för dig som är i början av din karriär och har ett stort intresse för system, struktur och affärsutveckling.


Vi söker efter en nyfiken, analytisk och initiativtagande person som vill vara med och vässa vår organisation till nästa nivå. Det här är inte en traditionell säljroll – det är rollen för dig som vill jobba bakom affären och göra den bättre, smartare och mer träffsäker.

**Om rollen**


I den här rollen blir du en drivkraft bakom mycket av vårt sälj- och uthyrningsarbete. Den som hittar möjligheter, bygger struktur och skapar insikter som driver resultat. Du tillhör avdelningen Servicecenter och rapporterar direkt till vår CXO.

**Du kommer bland annat att:**

* Utveckla våra säljprocesser genom smart analys och segmentering
* Dyka ner i data och omvandla den till konkreta affärsinsikter
* Bygga användarvänliga listor, modeller och uppföljning
* Identifiera vad som funkar (och inte), och föreslå förbättringar
* Ta egna initiativ och driva idéer från tanke till verklighet
* Bidra till välgrundade beslut genom att stötta CXO med datadriven analys och insikter

**Vem är du?**


Du är troligen i början av din karriär, men redan vass i hur du tänker och ser möjligheter i datadriven information. För att trivas i rollen och kunna leverera resultat har du ett starkt analytiskt mindset och gillar att lösa problem. Du är systemstark och jobbar naturligt med struktur och noggrannhet i alla arbetsuppgifter. Du både är och trivs med att vara självgående och väntar inte på att någon ska peka dig i en riktning, du agerar. Eftergymnasial utbildning är ett plus, men inget måste. Du behöver inte vara expert på BI-verktyg eller specifika system, men du ska gilla att lära dig och snabbt komma in i nya verktyg. Som person är du en proaktiv lagspelare som trivs i en roll med många kontaktytor och samarbetar smidigt med olika funktioner för att nå gemensamma mål.


Här får du chansen att påverka på riktigt. Du kommer inte bara analysera, du kommer förändra hur vi jobbar med uthyrning och försäljning. Dina idéer kommer märkas.
  

Skicka in din ansökan redan idag, vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!

**Vi erbjuder**


Läs mer om oss och vad vi erbjuder som arbetsgivare på vår hemsida.

**Övrig information**

* Start: Enligt överenskommelse
* Omfattning: Heltid
* Placering: Malmö, Västra hamnen
* Bakgrundskontroll ingår i rekryteringsprocessen

**Ansökan**


Vi går igenom ansökningar löpande, så skicka in din ansökan så snart som möjligt.  

Har du frågor? Kontakta vår CXO Annika Lantz på annika.lantz@victoriahem.se.

**Välkommen med din ansökan!**

title: Junior LabVIEW utvecklare (Lund)
description:
Vill du bygga system som möjliggör innovation? Tycker du om varierande uppdrag inom olika branscher och teknologiska fält? Är du intresserad av att arbeta på ett mindre, specialiserat konsultföretag där dina idéer värderas? Om så är fallet, tror vi att du skulle passa perfekt hos Prevas Test & Measurement! Vi vill erbjuda dig en inkluderande och stöttande arbetsmiljö där dina tekniska färdigheter och kunskaper kommer till sin rätt.

**Om rollen**

  

Som konsult hos oss kan du arbeta både onsite hos våra kunder eller i projekt på vårt kontor. Du blir en del av ett dynamiskt team där teamwork, kommunikation och kunskapsdelning är centrala i vårt arbete. Vi skapar lösningar som hjälper våra kunder att säkerställa högkvalitativa produkter och effektiv produktion från början till slut. Du kan därför bli involverad i en rad olika uppgifter, från att utveckla automatiserade system för kvalitetsbedömning till kravhantering och stakeholder management.


Som mätsystemsutvecklare kommer du att designa och implementera både mjukvaru- och hårdvarulösningar. Detta inkluderar att integrera elektronik, sensorer, mätinstrument och kontrollenheter med nyutvecklad mjukvara. Du kommer också att delta i utvecklingen av olika kompletta test- och mätsystem, från automatiserade testsystem för försvarsindustrin till mekaniska testriggar för fordonsindustrin och inspektionssystem för MedTech-applikationer.

 **Om dig**  

Du tycker om att dela med dig av din kunskap och att lära av andra. Du tar initiativ när det behövs och arbetar bra både självständigt och i team. Detta innebär att du är driven och kan ta ansvar för dina bidrag. Dessutom värdesätter du balansen mellan att lyssna på andra och att bidra med egna idéer, vilket gör dig till en mycket uppskattad kollega. Du drivs av nyfikenhet och motiveras av att hitta tekniska lösningar på mänsklighetens stora utmaningar.

* Du har en MSc inom elektroteknik, maskinteknik, teknisk fysik eller motsvarande kvalifikationer.
* Du har upp till 3 års arbetslivserfarenhet eller en doktorsgrad inom ett relevant område.
* Du har professionell erfarenhet av arbete som mjukvaruutvecklare eller besitter kunskaper på motsvarande nivå. Du är väl bevandrad i LabVIEW eller Python. Kunskaper i C#, C++, Julia eller Rust är meriterande.
* För just denna tjänst krävs svenskt medborgarskap samt goda kunskaper i svenska.

 **För att lyckas i denna roll är det bra att ha erfarenhet av en eller flera områden:**

* Test system design
* Interfacing hardware
* Control systems
* Software architecture
* Protocols such as CAN and Modbus
* Signal processing
* NI hardware
* Mechatronics
* Electrical motors
* Automation
* Sensors

 **Vårt erbjudande**


Hos Prevas Test & Measurement får du möjligheten till både professionell och personlig utveckling. Vi erbjuder flexibla arbetstider, stöttande ledarskap och regelbundna sociala aktiviteter som stärker vår företagsgemenskap. Våra projekt är utmanande och varierade; vi tar oss an allt som kan mätas fysiskaliskt och samarbetar med alla branscher som jobbar med hårdvara.

 **Vårt åtagande för hållbarhet**  

Hållbarhet är kärnan i vårt uppdrag. Vi prioriterar att arbeta med och stötta företag som utvecklar grön teknologi för att tackla miljöutmaningar. Som en del av vårt team kommer du att bidra till att skapa hållbara lösningar som överträffar våra kunders förväntningar.


Vi lägger stort fokus på att skapa en rolig och hållbar arbetsmiljö för alla våra medarbetare. I vår strävan efter en jämställd och inkluderande miljö har vi uppnått vårt mål om 40% kvinnliga medarbetare, vilket vi fortsätter arbeta för att bibehålla.

 **Ansök nu!**  

Har vi väckt ditt intresse? Skicka in din ansökan så snart som möjligt! Ansökningarna granskas löpande.


Sista ansökningsdag: 2026-05-31.

***Mer om Prevas Test & Measurement***  

*Prevas Test & Measurement (tidigare DVel) är specialiserade på hårdvarutestautomation och har vårt kontor i centrala Lund. Vi erbjuder konsulttjänster och/eller levererar kompletta mätsystem och testriggar. Våra projekt är mycket varierade och våra kunder finns inom flera olika branscher. För närvarande har vi 30 anställda och ser fram emot att växa med dig.*

*Sedan oktober 2023 är vi en del av**Prevas**. Vi är en affärsenhet inom Prevas South och kommer att fortsätta erbjuda expertis inom test och mätning.*

title: Senior iOS Developer
description:
**Why** **Doktor.se**
---------------------


Doktor.se is Sweden's largest digital healthcare provider. We combine physical clinics with a digital platform serving millions of consultations per year — backed by a profitable, international organization with 1,000+ employees and over 1B SEK in revenue. Our 25-person engineering team operates with startup autonomy inside enterprise stability.


We're actively integrating LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic) into patient and clinician experiences — and the mobile apps are where patients interact with all of it.

**The role**
------------


You'll join a small, high-ownership mobile team: you, our iOS lead, and two Android engineers working closely together across platforms.


This isn't a maintenance role. We're actively modernizing our iOS architecture, improving test coverage, and making real decisions about how we structure things going forward. You'll be shaping how we build iOS at Doktor.se — not just writing features to spec.

**What you'll work on**
-----------------------

* Help settle on a clean, sustainable architecture — we're evaluating approaches now — and drive the migration.
* Build new features end-to-end in collaboration with product managers and designers.
* Improve testing practices and ideally bring TDD into the team's workflow.
* Bring AI capabilities into the patient experience — we're expanding what we do on the platform into the mobile apps.
* Own the release process and deployment pipeline.
* Work with our Android team to ensure a coherent experience across platforms.

*Shape how we build iOS — not just execute tickets.*

**What we're looking for**
--------------------------

* 5+ years of professional iOS development.
* Solid understanding of architecture patterns — and opinions about what works in practice, not just in theory.
* Experience across both modern and legacy UI frameworks. Our codebase uses both, and you should be comfortable navigating that.
* Experience writing tests and ideally bringing TDD practices into a team.
* Comfortable integrating with backend services.
* Experience with modular codebases or breaking monoliths into modules.
* Experience improving and refactoring production codebases — not just greenfield projects.
* University degree in computer science, engineering, or equivalent experience.

**Who you are**
---------------

* You see tech debt as an opportunity, not a chore. You enjoy the work of making things better.
* You're senior enough to drive work independently, suggest improvements, and push back when needed — but you're not looking to be the lead. You want to build, not manage.
* You care about craft: clean code, good tests, thoughtful architecture. But you're also pragmatic about shipping.

**Tech stack**
--------------

**Language -** Swift **UI** SwiftUI, UIKit

**Architecture -** MVVM, Combine, Clean Architecture (evolving)

**Testing -** XCTest, UI testing

**CI/CD -** GitHub Actions, App Store Connect

**Backend integration** - REST APIs

**AI -** OpenAI, Anthropic (tooling & platform-wide, expanding to mobile)

**What we offer**
-----------------

* **Technical ownership.** You'll shape how iOS is built here, not just pick up tickets.
* **Direct impact.** Small team, millions of patients. Your work is visible immediately.
* **AI-forward environment.** We're running LLMs in production today — you'll bring that into the mobile experience.
* **Stable employer.** Profitable international healthcare organization, not a runway-burning startup.
* Collective agreement, 30 days vacation, ITP1 pension, wellness allowance, personal education budget.

**Practical details**
---------------------

* **Start:** As soon as possible. We'll accommodate your notice period.
* **Employment:** Permanent, full-time. Six month probation period.
* **Location:** On-site, Monday–Thursday at Sveavägen 63, Stockholm. Fridays are flexible.
* **Language:** English is our working language in tech. Swedish is a plus for collaborating with clinical and business teams, but not a hard requirement.
* **Work permit:** You must already have the right to work in Sweden.


We handle recruitment internally — no agencies, no consultants, no external recruiters.
company_description: På Doktor.Se vill vi göra saker annorlunda. Vi vill utmana och förändra en hel bransch.

title: Senior Apputvecklare som vill göra skillnad!
description:
**Vi söker en lösningsorienterad Apputvecklare till vårt team i Malmö!**


Vi söker nu en erfaren Apputvecklare som trivs i en produktorganisation där du får vara med att utveckla lösningar som gör skillnad för tusentals hemtjänstpersonal runt om i Sverige.


Du trivs med att samarbeta i team där kvalitet och kontinuerligt förbättring är centralt och du har ett genuint teknikintresse.


Är du dessutom nyfiken på att utveckla långsiktiga lösningar som bidrar till en tryggare och mer effektiv arbetsdag tillsammans med engagerade kolleger?


Då låter det som du skall du söka det här jobbet!

**Om produkten**


Alfa eCare Welfare är ett modernt IT-system som stöttar hemtjänsten i allt från planering och dokumentation till signering och avvikelsehantering. Systemet används idag av både kommunala och privata utförare och är känt för sin användarvänlighet, flexibilitet och förmåga att effektivisera vardagen för vård- och omsorgspersonal.


Vi jobbar med moduler för bland annat:

* Planering och ruttoptimering
* Dokumentation och journalföring enligt SoL, LSS och HSL
* Säker signering av insatser och läkemedelshantering
* Avvikelsehantering enligt Lex Sarah och Lex Maria

**Om rollen**


Vi söker en senior React Native-utvecklare som trivs i en verksamhetskritisk miljö. Du blir en del av ett tight team där samarbete, vardagskvalitet och nytta för användaren står i centrum. Vi ser det som självklart att du kan resonera kring lösningar och förklara tekniska beslut.


I rollen får du stort tekniskt ägandeskap och är med och påverkar arkitektur, tekniska val och hur vi bygger en skalbar app för framtiden. Du kommer att vara involverad i varje steg, från idé till utveckling men även förvaltning efter release. Vi ser att du kommer vara med att hitta nya lösningar och göra våra appar ännu bättre för våra användare.


Eftersom produkten är väletablerad men under ständig förbättring får du arbeta brett — både med långsiktiga tekniklyft och värdedriven nyutveckling, liksom vidareutveckling av befintliga delar där releaser, bugghantering och underhåll är naturliga inslag i vardagen.


Det här är en roll för dig som:

* Har flera års erfarenhet av att utveckla mobilappar och känner dig trygg i React Native för både iOS och Android.
* Vana att arbeta med publicering till App Store & Google Play
* Du är trygg i din kunskap men alltid ödmjuk och ivrig att lära dig mer.
* En vänlig kollega, eftertänksam i ditt sätt att arbeta och kommunicera med andra.
* Pratar och skriver flytande svenska, samt minst grundläggande kunskap i engelska.


Det är meriterande om du också har erfarenhet av backend-utveckling med C# och .NET

**Om oss**


Vi är en del av det nederländska bolaget SDB Zorgt, och tillsammans är vi cirka 500 medarbetare i Europa. I Norden är vi runt 140 kollegor med kontor i Sverige, Danmark och Finland.


Som del av en större koncern kombinerar vi stabilitet och långsiktighet med känslan av ett mindre bolag — med korta beslutsvägar, nära ledarskap och stort eget ansvar.


Vårt mål är att göra skillnad i offentlig sektor, med lösningar som hjälper vård och omsorg att fungera bättre. Vi är stolta över att vår kod faktiskt påverkar samhället, på riktigt**.**

**Vi erbjuder**

* Ett meningsfullt uppdrag – du bidrar till att förbättra svensk äldreomsorg
* Trygg arbetsgivare med stabila kunder och långsiktiga projekt
* Kollegor som bryr sig – om både produkten och varandra
* Möjligheter att växa och påverka, även i en mogen teknisk miljö
* Friskvårdsbidrag, samt en friskvårdstimme per vecka för att träna på arbetstid
* Privat sjukvårdsförsäkring
* Kollektivavtal, inklusive tjänstepension
* Flexibla arbetstider och möjlighet att arbeta på distans 1-2 dagar i veckan

**Övrigt**


Start: Snarast


Språk: Ansökan skrivs på svenska


Omfattning: Heltid, tillsvidare


Placering: Malmö

**Kontakt**


Har du frågor om rollen? Hör av dig till:  

Ingrid Gustafsson, Teamlead Providers  

ingrid.gustafsson@alfaecare.com

**Observera att vi inte är intresserade av kontakt från rekryteringsföretag eller konsultbolag.**

title: Systemutvecklare med fokus på e-tjänster
description:
Vi söker dig som vill vara med och forma morgondagens Ale. Hos oss är du med och levererar service och tjänster av bästa kvalitet till Aleborna. Tillsammans skapar 2 500 medarbetare ett Ale, kommunen som fokuserar på invånarna och deras behov. Bli en av oss!

### **Om arbetsplatsen**


Hos Ale kommun arbetar cirka 2 500 medarbetare tillsammans för att leverera service av hög kvalitet, med invånarnas behov i centrum. Bli en del av vårt team och bidra till att göra kommunen mer tillgänglig och digital.


Vi är en liten enhet där alla har ett varierat arbete där det gäller att kunna se nyttan och
  

helheten för våra användare. Våra uppgifter omfattar allt från den digitala arbetsplatsen för
  

kommunens elever och personal till att sköta drift och underhåll av nät och servrar. Dessutom bidrar vi till kommunens utveckling av digitala verktyg genom utvecklingsprojekt tillsammans med verksamheten.

### **Arbetsuppgifter**


Vill du skapa digitala lösningar som gör vardagen enklare för människor? Vi söker en engagerad systemutvecklare med intresse för användarvänlighet och UX till vår IT-enhet. Hos oss får du chansen att forma e-tjänster som verkligen används och uppskattas av Aleborna och kommunens medarbetare.


Tjänsten är **tidsbegränsad på 14 månader (men med möjlighet till förlängning)** och fokuserar på utveckling av våra e-tjänster med användaren i centrum. Du samarbetar nära verksamheten och kollegor och får möjlighet att påverka hur våra digitala tjänster upplevs.

**Exempel på arbetsuppgifter:**

* Utveckla och vidareutveckla e-tjänster med fokus på användarvänlighet
* Arbeta med integrationer via REST API:er
* Bidra med idéer som förbättrar användarupplevelsen och skapar värde för invånare


Vi är ett mindre team där alla hjälps åt – här får du variation och möjligheten att se helheten för våra användare.

**Teknik och arbetssätt**

* Plain JavaScript och webbutveckling
* Integrationer med REST API
* Agilt arbetssätt med fokus på användarnas behov


Du kommer att arbeta med att utveckling, implementering och förvaltning inom ditt område. Här jobbar du från start till mål tillsammans med verksamheten och kollegor.


På vår enhet arbetar vi tillsammans vilket innebär att dina arbetsuppgifter också kan omfatta andra områden och samarbete med kollegor som utvecklar andra delar av verksamheten.

### **Kvalifikationer**


För att klara uppdraget behöver du ha relevant utbildning, kunskaper och erfarenhet inom området. Vi ser att du ska kunna arbeta systematiskt i projektform och i samverkan med användare.


Vi tror att du:

* har relevant utbildning eller motsvarande erfarenhet inom systemutveckling
* har känsla för UX och användarcentrerad design
* gillar att arbeta nära användare och kollegor för att skapa bästa möjliga lösningar
* kan arbeta strukturerat, gärna i projektform


Erfarenhet av e-tjänster eller offentlig verksamhet är meriterande, men viktigast är att du vill göra tjänster som fungerar för människor.


Har du skyddade personuppgifter ska du INTE söka via systemet utan kontakta rekryteringsansvarig för hantering av din ansökan. För att underlätta rekryteringsarbetet ber vi dig att bifoga relevanta intyg och betyg för tjänsten du söker samt vid eventuell intervju ha möjlighet att uppvisa giltig ID-handling. Vi har endast möjlighet att ta emot ansökningar via Varbi. Vi strävar efter en jämn könsfördelning och ser mångfald som en styrka och välkomnar därför sökande med olika bakgrund. Vill du veta mer om hur det är att arbeta i Ale kommun? Läs mer här: Jobba i Ale kommun | Ale kommun Inför rekryteringsarbetet har vi tagit ställning till rekryteringskanaler och marknadsföring. Vi undanber oss därför bestämt kontakt med mediesäljare, rekryteringssajter och liknande. Vi använder e-rekrytering för att effektivisera och kvalitetssäkra rekryteringsarbetet. Alla dina uppgifter sparas på ditt CV-konto och du kan enkelt logga in och uppdatera dina uppgifter när du vill. Fyll därför i dina uppgifter så omsorgsfullt som möjligt för att ge en rättvisande bild av dig själv. Dina personuppgifter behandlas i Varbi under rekryteringsprocessen och i den utsträckning detta är nödvändigt för att uppfylla lagstiftningen. Ansökan raderas 24 månader efter avslutande av rekrytering. Om du har ett Varbi-konto kan du använda redigeringen av din profil för att radera, ladda upp och redigera information. Kontakta annars Ale kommun. Vi vill upplysa om att din ansökan är en offentlig handling enligt offentlighetsprincipen.


Anställningsform: särskild visstidsanställning | Anställningens omfattning: heltid | Antal lediga befattningar: 1 | Sysselsättningsgrad: 100% | Ort: Ale | Län: Västra Götalands län | Land: Sweden | Referensnummer: 2026/136 | Kontakt: Kristina Bilger 0702-736679, | Facklig företrädare: Fackliga representanter nås via Kontaktcenter 0303-703000, | Publicerat: 2026-03-19 | Sista ansökningsdag: 2026-04-19

title: Full Stack Software Engineer – New Graduate
description:
**Appostrophe is a mobile app development company creating easy-to-use design tools that unlock people's creativity. Our top-listed app, SCRL, is used by millions of people worldwide.** 




  





 We're a focused and ambitious team built on collaboration and transparency. We're now looking for a recent graduate to join us and grow into a world-class full stack engineer.
 



  





**About the role** 




 We're looking for a new graduate who wants to grow as an engineer while being part of a high-speed startup team shipping a creativity app to millions of users. In this role, you will support the development of our web-based editor and the backend systems that power SCRL across iOS, Android, and Web, while working with senior engineers to learn and grow your skills. This is a great opportunity to gain hands-on experience and contribute to a cutting-edge product!
 



  





**What you will do** 



* Work alongside our experienced full stack team to implement and ship new features across our web editor and backend
* Collaborate closely with product and design to bring ideas to life
* Write clean, maintainable code in React, TypeScript, and Node.js
* Learn from real-time user feedback and usage data to continuously improve the product
* Participate in code reviews and technical discussions as part of a supportive engineering team



  





**Who you are** 



* A recent graduate, or soon to graduate, in Computer Science, Software Engineering, IT, Engineering Physics or a related field
* Have a solid understanding of modern software development and great programming skills
* Passionate about user experience and building things people love to use
* Excited to learn, grow and take more ownership over time
* Bonus if you've built web apps or worked on backend projects in the past



  





**We offer** 



* A unique opportunity to learn from experienced engineers and make an impact from day one
* Freedom to be creative and test new ideas
* Great colleagues and office in central Stockholm
* Competitive compensation and opportunities for professional development



  





 If you're excited about full stack development, and want to grow as an engineer while shipping products to millions of users, we'd love to hear from you!

title: Fullstack Engineer
description:
At Hemnet, we build one of Sweden’s most widely used digital services. Every day, we help people navigate some of life’s biggest decisions and that brings high expectations on quality, reliability, and long-term thinking.
   

  

 We’re now looking for a Fullstack Engineer to join our Seller Domain.The Seller Domain builds products and services that help homeowners make the most of their opportunities in the housing market.
   

  

**About The Role 🍏** 
  

  

 You’ll be part of an experienced, cross-functional team working across both user-facing features and backend systems. You’ll contribute through hands-on development, technical leadership and close collaboration with Product Managers, Designers, Analysts, and Engineers. Together, you’ll build solutions that create real value for sellers.
   

  

 You’ll design, build, and maintain scalable backend services using Node.js and Nest.js, contribute to frontend development in React, and support and evolve APIs across GraphQL and REST. You’ll also play an active role in our transition toward a more service-oriented architecture, helping break down complex problems and move monolithic systems into modular services over time.
   

  

 Beyond day-to-day development, you’ll take part in technical discussions, contribute to architectural direction, support other engineers through collaboration and knowledge sharing, and help us continuously improve code quality, performance, and developer experience. We also hope you’ll contribute to a team culture where different perspectives are valued and people can grow together.
   

  

**Who we think you are** 
 🤩
   

  

 Our current stack includes Ruby and Node.js on the backend and Next.js on the frontend. You’ll work with Ruby in this role, but we don’t expect you to know everything from the start.
   

  

 We think you’re an experienced engineer who enjoys building production-grade systems and working through complex problems together with others. You care about code quality, maintainability, and creating solutions that hold up over time.
   

  

 You’re comfortable contributing to technical decisions, and you like collaborating across disciplines to build products that are useful and thoughtful. You don’t need to tick every box on day one, what matters most is that you bring solid engineering experience, a learning mindset, and a generous way of working with others.
   

  

**What You’ll Do 💥** 
  

  

* Build and improve Hemnet’s products across the system.
* Take part in technical discussions and architectural decisions
* Work closely with product, design, and analytics.
* Contribute to a sustainable pace, high quality, and a great developer experience.
* You’ll use AI tools as part of your day-to-day work, for example to explore ideas, speed up implementation, improve code quality, and support learning and collaboration across the team.


  

  

**I am your new manager 👋** 
  

  

 My name is Johan Boberg, I joined Hemnet in June, prior to Hemnet I worked at Blocket for many years. I have a long career behind me as Engineering Manager and before that, as Backend Developer.
   

  

 As a manager, I am supportive and humble, my goal is to create an environment where you can feel trusted, be challenged and empowered to grow. My job is to lead the team to a productive and creative space where everyone can contribute.
   

  

**A Note on Inclusion 💚** 
  

  

 At Hemnet, we believe that diversity in gender, sexual orientation, ability, neurodiversity, cultural background and life experience makes us stronger. We welcome applicants of all identities and backgrounds.
   

  

 If you meet many but not all of the requirements, we encourage you to apply. We value curiosity, a strong sense of purpose and the motivation to learn and grow, alongside your skills and experiences.
   

  

**A Hybrid Way of Working 🚲** 
  

  

 Our vibrant office in central Stockholm is the hub of our collaboration and culture, providing a lively space that fosters team spirit. We come together in the office three days a week, with two flexible remote days. To be part of our team, it’s essential to be based in or near Stockholm.

title: Back End Developer
description:
**Senior Backend Engineer (AI Product)🚀** 




  





 CodeX Digital are currently supporting a fast-growing AI start-up in Stockholm, who are building an AI-powered consumer-grade app experience & they’re looking for a strong Backend Engineer to join the team🚀
 



  





 This is an opportunity to work on a highly interactive platform where real-time data, AI models, and game-like logic come together to create engaging user experiences. You’ll join a small, high-impact team building systems that evolve quickly, ship often, and directly influence how the product feels and performs at scale⭐️
 



  





**What You'll Do🔧** 



* Design, build and scale backend systems powering a real time, data driven product
* Develop APIs, connect LLMs, internal services & external platforms
* Help shape the architecture behind AI-native and agent driven experiences
* Collaborate closely with product, frontend and AI teams



  





**What We're Looking For🌟** 



* Strong backend engineering experience
* Deeply comfortable with Node.js & TypeScript (Python is a plus)
* Experience integrating or building with foundational AI models
* Startup energy and likes shipping fast
* Builds AI side projects or experiments out of curiosity



  





**What You'll Get In Return** 
 💥
 


* Competitive compensation + equity
* High-impact role shaping the future of an AI product from early stage
* Collaboration with top-tier engineers
* Clear growth opportunities as the company scales
* Hybrid - 3 days a week onsite (Central Stockholm)



  





**Note:** 




 We're looking for both Mid + Senior Engineers to join the team so please don't hesitate to apply if you meet the criteria and are not Senior!

title: Fullstack Developer (Frontend focused)
description:
**The Mission**
 Backlight is a global media technology company dedicated to improving the entire video content lifecycle, from creation to monetization. Our suite of SaaS solutions empowers creative teams at every stage of production and is trusted by video-forward organizations like Google, Ubisoft, the Atlanta Hawks, and Marvel.
   

  

 We’re looking for an engineer who leads with the frontend but owns features end-to-end—from the interface down to the API layer. You’ll spend the vast majority of your time crafting fast, intuitive React applications, while also having the autonomy to write and extend the Python services that power them.
   

  

 You’ll join a cross-functional product team alongside designers, PMs, and backend specialists, shaping the user experience while having the technical freedom to reach into the stack whenever your features need it.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Build and maintain scalable, reliable frontend applications using React, TypeScript, and modern JavaScript.
* Design responsive, accessible, and performant user interfaces that work beautifully across devices.
* Write and extend backend services in Python when your features require it — building endpoints, updating data models, and connecting the dots between frontend and backend.
* Collaborate closely with backend, product, and design teams to integrate APIs and bring features to life.
* Help diagnose, troubleshoot, and solve real production issues across the stack.
* Bring new ideas around tooling, frameworks, architecture, and ways of working — your creativity matters here.


**Requirements**
 We believe great engineers can learn new tools as they go — what matters most is your ability and genuine drive to build great user experiences and collaborate well. In addition, we believe the right person has:
   

  

* 3+ years of experience building and shipping scalable web applications.
* Strong command of React, TypeScript, and modern JavaScript patterns.
* Comfortable working across the stack, including REST APIs, Git workflows, and light backend development in Python or a similar language.
* Experience writing clean, structured, testable, and maintainable code.
* Strong communication skills — you collaborate openly and value teamwork.
* Knowledge of CSS frameworks and modern styling approaches.


**Bonus points**
* Experience building SaaS products, mentoring peers or Scrum facilitation.


**Work Arrangement**
**Location:** 
 Stockholm
   

  

**Time zone:** 
 CET

title: Junior Product Developer – H&M Denim Womenswear
description:
**Job Description**

*Are you passionate about fashion, trends and the idea of creating an assortment that inspire and elevate the everyday wardrobe?* 
*We are looking for a Junior Product Developer with a strategic mindset, a passion for denim, and a collaborative spirit to join our Denim team at H&M Womenswear.*




*This is a full-time temporary role, based at our HQ in Stockholm.* 
  

  





**WHAT YOU’LL DO** 
  

  

 We’re looking for a Junior Product Developer who’s eager to learn and motivated by teamwork and creativity. You’ll be part of a fast-paced environment where you’ll build experience in product development while contributing to an offer that inspires our global customer. As part of the Denim team at H&M Womenswear, you’ll help shape a relevant and attractive assortment based on trend insights, customer needs and commercial opportunities. Working closely with the Product Developer, you’ll support the development process from idea to final product. Your passion for denim and your drive to make things happen will be essential in creating high‑quality on-trend products.
   

  





 You’ll collaborate with a Product Manager, Designers, Planners and other key functions to ensure strong alignment and smooth coordination throughout the development flow. You’ll be a central link in the daily operations, handling multiple tasks in parallel and supporting the work with samples, product follow-up and preparation for upcoming collections.
   

  





 In this role you’ll contribute to developing denim that meet customer expectations and strengthen our commercial potential, always guided by our strategy and targets.
   

  





  

  





**WHO YOU ARE**




 We are looking for people with
   

  




* 2+ years of experience from a similar role as Product Developer or Buyer
* Denim knowledge
* Education in Textiles, Buying or Product Development
* A keen eye for current fashion trends with strong fashion intelligence
* A commercial mindset with a strong drive to contribute to business growth


* A structured and organized way of working, with the ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously


* A collaborative and communicative approach, thriving in a cross functional environment with a humble and hands on attitude


* A curious mindset and eagerness to continuously learn and develop



  

  





**WHO WE ARE** 
**​** 





 H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​
   

  





 We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​
   

  





  

  





**JOIN US** 






 Additional information:
   

  

 This is a full time, temporary position with placement in Stockholm. If you feel your experience, skills and ambitions are right for this role, please send your application including CV in English as soon as possible. Interviews will be held continuously. (For internal candidates, please initiate a dialogue with you manager before applying.)  If you are currently in a permanent employment with H&M you will keep your permanent employment even if you take on a temporary position for a limited time.
   

  





  

  





**Benefits:** 
  

  

 At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer all our employees at H&M Group attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. All our employees receive a staff discount card, usable on all our H&M Group brands in stores and online. Brands covered by the discount are H&M (Beauty and Move included), COS, Weekday, Monki, H&M HOME, & Other Stories, ARKET. In addition to our staff discount, all our employees are included in our H&M Incentive Program – HIP.
   

  





  

  





**Inclusion & Diversity:**
  

 H&M is a part of H&M Group. At H&M Group, we’re determined to create and maintain inclusive, diverse and equitable workplaces throughout our organisation. Our teams should consist of a variety of people that share and combine their knowledge, experience and ideas. Having a diverse workforce leads to a positive impact on how we address challenges, on what we perceive possible and on how we choose to relate to our colleagues and customers all over the world. Hence all diversity dimensions are taken into consideration in our recruitment process.
   

  





 We strive to have a fair and equal process and therefore kindly ask you not to attach a cover letter in your application as they often contain information that easily can trigger unintentional biases.
   

  





  

  





**Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.**

title: Dotnet Developer
description:
**Role – Application Support Engineer** 




**Primary Skillset: .Net, L2/L3 support, ITIL processes and ITSM tools (ServiceNow or equivalent), Monitoring & observability tools (APM, logs, alerts)** 




**Location – Stockholm, Sweden** 




**Duration- Permanent** 




  





**Job Description** 




**Your role** 




 In the role of Application Support Engineer, you will be responsible for proactive identification of issues in production environment leveraging observability tools with the aim of reducing MTTD and MTTR. In the process, you will also be collaborating and working closely with business, product and engineering teams, across geographies. You will be part of a learning culture, where teamwork and collaboration are encouraged, excellence is rewarded, and diversity is respected and valued.
 



**Responsibilities** 



* Interfacing with technical and business stakeholders – internal and external (Managers / Application owners, Support Analysts, Infrastructure Team, Developers and technology specialists)
* Managing and maintaining production environment
* Change management and application deployments
* Restore a normal service operation as quickly as possible and minimize the impact on business operations, thus ensuring that the best possible levels of service quality and availability are maintained.
* Coordinate all activities required to lead incident triage in compliance with SLAs and OLAs.
* Facilitate the outage calls and ensure that all the required resources are engaged to work a Major Incident.
* Preparing internal reports on Major incidents and leading their review with Executive Management.
* SLA & KPI reporting
* Onsite – Offshore coordination
* Flexibility to work in shifts/on call support environment



**Required** 



* Strong experience in application support and maintenance (tier 2 and tier 3)
* Incident management & problem management
* Change management and production deployments
* Batch job management & production
* Dashboarding, automation and simplification
* Application transition and steady state support
* SLA & KPI management
* SOX Compliance activities

title: Product Developer Heavy Woven - H&M Womenswear
description:
**Job Description**

*Are you passionate about fashion, driven by results and excited about turning creative ideas into commercial success?
   

  

 We are looking for a Product Developer with a strategic mindset, a strong passion for heavy woven trousers and a collaborative spirit to join our Womenswear team at H&M.
   

  

 This is a full‑time, temporary position based at our HQ in Stockholm, Sweden.*




**WHAT YOU’LL DO​**
  

 As a Product Developer within Heavy Woven, you will play a key role in shaping the H&M Womenswear trousers assortment. You will set and drive both short and long‑term plans for your category, ensuring alignment with our overall assortment direction.
   

  

 With a strong commercial mindset, deep product knowledge within heavy woven trousers and the ability to translate trends into relevant opportunities, you will identify seasonal keys and act fast on what’s emerging. You will continuously refine our product and component work to build a strong, competitive and customer‑centred trousers offer.
   

  

 You’ll take ideas from concept to final product, driving development forward and ensuring smooth cross‑functional collaboration in a fast‑moving environment. You will work closely with a product manager, planner, designer and pattern maker, and partner with our production team to bring each piece to life with the right quality, fit and feel.
   

  

 Your passion for heavy woven products and your ability to make things happen will be essential in creating high‑quality, on‑trend trousers that resonate with our customers.
   

  





**WHO YOU ARE**
  

 Qualifications:
   

  




* Five or more years of experience in a similar role as Product Developer or Buyer
* Experience working with heavy woven garments, preferably trousers
* Education in textiles, buying or product development
* A proven track record of creating products for the womenswear customer with excellent fashion sensibility
* Strong fashion intelligence and a keen eye for current trends
* A deep passion for products and product development
* Driven, structured and proactive in taking initiatives
* Strong salesmanship with a results‑oriented mindset
* Excellent communication skills
* A strong team player with a flexible, solution‑driven approach
* Ability to identify opportunities, improvements and efficient ways of working



**WHO WE ARE**
  

 H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.
   

  

 We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future.
   

  





**JOIN US​**
  

 Additional information:
   

  

**This is a full-time, temporary position, with placement in Stockholm.** 
 If you feel your experience, skills and ambitions are right for this role, please send your application including
 **CV in English** 
 as soon as possible. Interviews will be held continuously. (For internal candidates, please initiate a dialogue with you manager before applying.)  If you are currently in a permanent employment with H&M you will keep your permanent employment even if you take on a temporary position for a limited time.
   

  





 Benefits:
   

  

 At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer all our employees at H&M Group attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. All our employees receive a staff discount card, usable on all our H&M Group brands in stores and online. Brands covered by the discount are H&M (Beauty and Move included), COS, Weekday, Monki, H&M HOME, & Other Stories, ARKET. In addition to our staff discount, all our employees are included in our H&M Incentive Program – HIP.
   

  





 Inclusion & Diversity:
   

  

 H&M is a part of H&M Group. At H&M Group, we’re determined to create and maintain inclusive, diverse and equitable workplaces throughout our organisation. Our teams should consist of a variety of people that share and combine their knowledge, experience and ideas. Having a diverse workforce leads to a positive impact on how we address challenges, on what we perceive possible and on how we choose to relate to our colleagues and customers all over the world. Hence all diversity dimensions are taken into consideration in our recruitment process.
   

  





 We strive to have a fair and equal process and therefore kindly ask you not to attach a cover letter in your application as they often contain information that easily can trigger unintentional biases.
   

  





 ​
   

  





**Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.​**

title: Back End Developer
description:
Vill du vara med och utveckla affärssystem tillsammans med ett engagerat team? EP Bank söker just nu en Backend utvecklare till deras IT-avdelningen!
 



  





**Om EP Bank** 




 EP Bank är en fristående, serviceinriktad och privatägd bank som erbjuder fastighetsfinansiering, rörelse- och förvärvsfinansiering, kredit- och finansieringssamarbeten samt sparkonton. EP Bank har ett 40-tal anställda och kontor i Stockholm. Deras affärskultur bygger på en övertygelse om att långsiktig framgång för banken endast kan vara grundad på framgång för våra kunder och uppdragsgivare.
 



  





**Om Rollen:** 




 Som backendutvecklare hos EP Bank arbetar du nära både teknik och affär. Du utvecklar i C#/.NET med fokus på att förbättra och vidareutveckla bankens egenutvecklade system, samt integrationer med stöd- och tredjepartssystem, allt kopplat till kärnverksamheten. Rollen innebär att du stärker systemen för kredit och inlåning, och bidrar med tekniska lösningar som skapar tydlig affärsnytta.
 



 Som en mindre bank är beslutsvägarna korta och förändringstakten hög. Det innebär att du snabbt kan omsätta idéer till förbättringar, med fokus på stabila system och lösningar som möter verksamhetens behov.
 



**Vidare ansvarsområden:** 



* Driva digitalisering som effektiviserar och utvecklar bankens affärsprocesser
* Ta gemensamt ansvar i teamet för kodkvalitet – från utveckling till test, uppföljning och förbättring
* Arbeta nära verksamheten för att förstå behov och omsätta dem till tekniska lösningar



  





**Om dig:** 




 Vi söker dig som är pålitlig, självgående och har viljan att förstå affären. Du blir en central del av vårt utvecklarteam, där du tillsammans med affärsansvariga skapar lösningar för våra kärnsystem. Du trivs i ett team där idéer diskuteras öppet och lösningarna ständigt förbättras - från idé till implementering.
 



  





**Relevant bakgrund:** 



* Gedigen erfarenhet av backend utveckling i centrala kärnsystem
* Djup kompetens i C#/.Net
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av att förvandla idéer och arkitektur till fungerande lösningar
* Erfarenhet av Azure, API-utveckling, relations- och dokumentdatabaser
* Flytande i svenska och engelska, både tal och skrift
* Erfarenhet inom bank, finans eller annan reglerad verksamhet är meriterande



  





***EP Bank jobbar tillsammans med Safemind som rekryteringspartner för denna rekrytering.***

title: Agentic Fullstack Developer
description:
At VetFamily, we're building the next generation of veterinary platforms - and AI agents are at the core of how we work and what we ship. We're looking for a Senior Fullstack Developer who doesn't just use AI tools to code faster, but who can design, build, and orchestrate AI agents that automate workflows, make decisions, and deliver real value across our B2B2C platform.
 



 This is a role for someone who sees agentic AI not as a buzzword but as an engineering discipline - and who has the fullstack skills to turn autonomous AI workflows into production-grade products serving pet owners, veterinary clinics, and suppliers across Europe, the US, and Australia.
 



  




 About the Role
 
 As a Fullstack Developer at VetFamily, you'll work across
 **Django** 
 on the backend and
 **React** 
 on the frontend, building scalable, reliable applications that serve thousands of clinics and their end customers. You'll implement business logic with the help of a strong PO and Agentic AI. A big part of the product is integrate with third-party services including
 **payment solutions** 
 .
 



 We're looking for someone who understands how systems fit together - someone equally comfortable modeling data and designing REST APIs as they are discussing with a support team to get new API keys.
 



  





  




 What You'll Do
 * **Build Agentic Systems** 
 – Design and implement AI agent workflows that automate real business processes—from data ingestion and decision-making to customer-facing actions. Think multi-step loops, tool use, memory, and human-in-the-loop patterns.
* **Own the Full Stack** 
 – Build features end-to-end: Django backends, React frontends, and the agentic layers in between. No artificial boundaries.
* **Integrate Payment Solutions** 
 – Implement and maintain payment provider integrations, handling multi-party transaction flows in a B2B2C context.
* **Ship With AI, Build for AI** 
 – Use Cursor, Claude Code, and other AI-powered dev tools daily. But go beyond personal productivity—build the agents that become part of the product itself.
* **Collaborate Across Borders** 
 – Work with design, product, data, and AI teams across Europe.



  




 You Might Be a Fit If...
 * You are social and like to participate in our office challenges and afterwork activities.
* You have
 **deep experience with Fullstack Development with external Data sources** 
 .
* You understand the
 **agentic AI stack** 
 : LLM APIs, function/tool calling, MCP, and how to evaluate and observe agent behavior.
* You've worked with
 **payment integrations** 
 (Stripe, Adyen, Klarna, or similar) and understand transaction flows, webhooks, and reconciliation.
* You see
 **AI-augmented development** 
 as your default mode—Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot are part of your daily toolkit, and you're constantly pushing what's possible.
* You thrive on
 **ownership** 
 and move fast from concept to production with a pragmatic, iterative approach.
* You're curious, collaborative, and genuinely excited about the intersection of
 **software engineering and AI agents** 
 .



  




 Apply Now
 
 Help us build the future of veterinary care - where AI agents and great engineering come together to create products that actually matter for Pet Owners, veterinarians and for our self.
 



 And hey - bring your dog to office!

title: ServiceNow Architect
description:
**At Alleima, ServiceNow is a strategic platform designed with a out-of-the-box architecture with minimal legacy dependencies making it possible to work proactive with stakeholders and product owners to implement new features and apply a forward-looking mindset. In recent years, we have implemented several core modules, and the** 
**roadmap ahead** 
**focuses on expanding capabilities, driving automation, and accelerating value creation.**
**As a ServiceNow Architect, you will join a dedicated team responsible for enabling efficient processes, modern technologies, and strong governance across the company. Now is the perfect time to strengthen our internal team as we advance towards a unified, scalable, and highly automated ServiceNow platform that supports Alleima’s long-term‑ vision.**
 Placement: Flexible within Sweden.
   

  

**Your Role**
 In the role as a ServiceNow Architect you will define and maintain the overall solution architecture, ensuring that all implementations are technically robust, aligned with ServiceNow best practices, and supported by clear, high-quality architectural documentation. You will take ownership of the platform’s end-to-end architecture, working both strategically and operationally across design, implementation, and continuous improvement. The role requires close collaboration with the platform owner, business stakeholders, IT teams, developers, and external partners to ensure that solutions are scalable, secure, and aligned with long-term objectives.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Design and plan ServiceNow solutions, including custom applications and integrations that meet business needs and industry standards.
* Stay current with ServiceNow developments and proactively introduce new ideas and improvements.
* Collaborate closely with stakeholders to translate business requirements into solid technical designs.
* Guide and support developers to ensure successful delivery of ServiceNow projects.
* Assess and evaluate existing implementations, providing clear recommendations for optimization.
* Ensure the platform remains secure, scalable, and easy to maintain through well-structured architectural governance.


**Profile**
 We believe you hold a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or have equivalent practical experience. You bring several years of hands-on experience working with the ServiceNow platform and are comfortable working end-to-end, from understanding business needs to delivering smart, scalable solutions. Your background may be as an architect, system administrator, or platform developer. You also hold relevant ServiceNow certifications such as ITSM, ITOM, or HRSD, and have solid knowledge of CSDM, software asset management, and building applications and integrations.
   

  

 As a person, you are driven, curious, humble, and a true team player. You enjoy learning, take initiative, and understand when a “good enough” solution creates the most value. You communicate clearly, build strong relationships across both business and IT, and navigate global stakeholders with ease. With your ability to balance quick wins and long-term thinking, you create alignment and help move discussions forward. You are fluent in English, and the ability to communicate in Swedish is a plus. For this position, you must be based in Sweden.
   

  

**What You Can Expect From Us**
* Be a part of a strong structured team devoted to ServiceNow in a creative and fast paced environment
* Deepen your knowledge of ServiceNow through continuous training and certifications
* Grow professionally and individually through interactions with stakeholders and solving their needs and requirements, as well as celebrating the success of these achievements in the team


**Other Information**
*We do not wish to be contacted by recruitment agencies or other sales and consulting companies regarding this position.* 
*In connection with employment, alcohol and drug tests are carried out according to our procedures for a safe working environment*
**Are you interested in learning more about this exciting position? Please contact:**
 Tobias Lundberg, recruiting manager, +46 (0)76 110 19 33
   

  

**For more information and questions about the recruitment process, please contact:**
 Sara Kühner, Recruiter in Charge, +46 76 495 02 40
   

  

**Union Contacts**
 Maria Sundqvist, Akademikerföreningen, 070 651 73 81
   

  

 Kjell-Åke Klockervold, Ledarna, 070 314 24 43
   

  

 Mikael Larsson, Unionen, 070 307 30 48
   

  

**You are welcome to submit your application no later than 2026-04-12**
 At Alleima, our mission is much more than delivering high-quality products, technology and processes - through collaboration, we develop the best solutions according to our customers' needs and that is how we achieve our business goals is the best way to describe our daily work. With curious employees and safety as our priority, we create a work environment where you can develop both as a person and in your work. With a clear direction for our journey, where we leverage our position as a technology leader, progressive business partner and where we are a driver in terms of sustainability, we aim to become an even stronger company in our industry. Are you ready to take on this challenge with us? Join us on our journey! www.alleima.com

title: Senior iOS Developer
description:

To date, Ubiquiti has shipped over 100 million devices worldwide, from ISP networking products to next generation of IT solutions. ***Please note that a background check will be conducted in the final stage of the recruitment process***

title: Engineering Manager
description:
Mentimeter is an engagement tool with a clear goal in mind. To turn presentations into conversations. Through real-time interactivity and clear visualizations, we get people to participate, engage and become more productive. Transforming all those passive meetings, airless classrooms and drawn out trainings into valuable and memorable moments.
   

  

 We truly believe that you achieve the best results by doing things together. And that successful leaders need to adopt a curious and collaborative mindset in order to get there. So with you at Mentimeter, you’ll be a big part of the ambition to help over 1 billion people listen, learn and work better together.
   

  

**The role**
 Mentimeter is looking for an Engineering Manager to join and raise the level of engineering craft across teams.
   

  

 This is not a delivery management role. It is a craft leadership role with with 10+ direct reports, while staying close to engineering work.
   

  

 Your primary responsibility is to grow engineers into high-performing product engineers, people who write high-quality code, design robust systems, make sound technical trade-offs, and understand the impact of their work on users and the business.
   

  

 You bring recent, hands-on engineering experience and strong technical judgment. You don’t need to contribute production code daily, but you are expected to go deep: reviewing code and system design, challenging decisions, and coaching engineers through real technical problems. You set the bar for what “good” looks like, and you help others reach it.
   

  

 You primarily work across teams, rather than being embedded in a single team. Through 1:1s, performance conversations, and collaboration with Staff Engineers and Product Managers, you identify patterns, raise standards, and drive continuous improvement in how we build software.
   

  

 Delivery is owned by teams. Your focus is capability: ensuring engineers grow, craft improves, and teams become more autonomous and effective over time.
   

  

 You’ll play a key role in shaping how product engineering evolves at Mentimeter, from defining craft excellence to embedding AI-augmented ways of working into everyday engineering practice.
   

  

**This role is not for you if**
* You prefer to stay far from code and technical discussions
* You see engineering management primarily as delivery or project management
* You are not comfortable reviewing code, discussing system design, or challenging decisions
* You want to own delivery execution or be the central coordinator for a team
* You prefer stable environments over continuous improvement and evolving practices


**The team**
 You’ll be working closely with Product Managers, Designers, Staff Engineers, and other Engineering Managers.
   

  

 Our teams are autonomous and cross-functional, owning their product areas end-to-end. As an Engineering Manager, you operate across these teams, supporting engineers in their growth, identifying patterns and opportunities across teams, and strengthening how we build products together.
   

  

 The work spans multiple dimensions: coaching individuals, improving engineering practices, supporting hiring and calibration, and helping engineers adopt a stronger product engineering mindset. You’ll collaborate closely with peers to shape the craft agenda and ensure we continuously raise the bar.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Coach and develop engineers through regular 1:1s, feedback, and clear growth plans
* Own performance management for your reports, including calibration and compensation input
* Challenge decisions around system design to ensure high standards in quality, scalability, and maintainability
* Identify recurring technical challenges across teams and drive improvements
* Partner with Staff Engineers to define and evolve engineering standards
* Help engineers connect technical decisions to user and business outcomes
* Support teams in adopting modern development practices, including AI-augmented workflows
* Contribute to hiring by attracting, assessing, and closing strong engineering talent


**Example Impact In This Role Could Include**
* Raising system reliability across multiple teams through better observability practices
* Coaching engineers through a major architectural shift or scaling challenge
* Improving code quality and review practices, leading to fewer production issues


**Who You Are**
 You are an experienced engineer who has stayed close to the craft and built a strong technical foundation. You have worked on real systems, made trade-offs in production, and understand what high-quality engineering looks like in practice.
   

  

 Today, you combine that technical depth with a passion for developing others. You care about raising the bar, not just for individuals, but across teams, and you know how to turn abstract “quality” into concrete, observable improvements.
   

  

 You are comfortable going deep in technical discussions, giving actionable feedback on engineering work, and coaching engineers through complex challenges. You bring strong judgment in areas like system design, code quality, and ways of working.
   

  

 You think beyond implementation. You help engineers understand problem spaces, make better trade-offs, and connect their work to real outcomes.
   

  

 You communicate clearly, build trust, and know how to balance supporting individuals with driving broader craft improvements across an Area.
   

  

**It’s a big plus if you have**
* Recent experience as a software engineer, tech lead, or similar role working on production systems
* Experience defining and raising engineering standards across teams
* Strong understanding of modern engineering practices (testing strategies, observability, scalable systems, developer experience)
* Experience supporting teams in adopting new technologies and ways of working, including AI-assisted development
* Experience hiring and calibrating engineers with a strong focus on craft
* Experience working in a product-led SaaS environment


**Not Required**
 Swedish is not required – daily work is carried out in English, and the Mentimeter team currently boasts over 50 nationalities. A college degree is not strictly required.
   

  

**Practical information**
 This role is based in our Stockholm office and is primarily on-site, with the possibility to work from home twice a week.
   

  

**Recruitment process**
* Introduction call
* Culture Leadership Interview
* Leadership / Competence Interview


**What Mentimeter Can Offer**
 At Mentimeter we can offer a diverse and inclusive work environment supported by smart and driven colleagues. We believe in continuous professional development for all of our colleagues and therefore offer access to a leadership program (including external personal coach) and relevant education to ensure that we continue to be state-of-the-art when it comes to innovating and building Mentimeter. Your place will be in a growing company with lots of career opportunities, working on a beloved product used by more than 300 million people. It’s not all about work though, we also offer a very healthy view on work-life balance.
   

  

 All of this comes attached with a competitive compensation and benefits package, including pension contributions. Learn more about our benefits by visiting our Benefits & Perks page
   

  

**AI and Hiring at Mentimeter**
 At Mentimeter, we believe AI helps us work smarter - but it never replaces the human assessment, curiosity, and personal connection that define our culture and our hiring. We use AI as a sparring partner: to bounce ideas, bring new perspectives, support structure, and make our work more efficient. But the meaning, decisions, and interactions always come from people.
   

  

* AI does not screen or decide on candidates.
* There is no automated filtering, ranking, or decision-making in our recruitment process. Every application is reviewed by a person.
* Hiring teams may use AI to support their work - for example, to structure notes, prepare interview questions, or organize their thinking.


 AI strengthens our work, but it does not define it. At Mentimeter, we’re not building an AI-driven hiring process - we’re building a people-first culture, where technology helps us listen, learn, and grow together.
   

  

**Culture at Mentimeter**
 At Mentimeter we believe in giving everyone a voice - regardless of who you are. So we build a platform that does just that. Our platform is not only our product but also our organization. A platform where people feel safe, where differences are embraced, a place where you can have fun. We strongly encourage applicants who are people of color, LGBTQ+, women, people with disabilities, and/or formerly incarcerated people, and a college degree is not strictly required. In order to give everyone a voice, we need to be as diverse as our users.
   

  

 Learn more about our culture by visiting our Culture page.
   

  

 Review our Privacy Policy for more information.

title: Credit Risk Model Developer, LGD Models [Regular/Senior]
description:
**Job ID: 2606**
**Quantitative Risk Analyst, LGD Models [Regular/Senior] (permanent position)**
 Do you want to play an important role in one of the largest model development programmes in Nordics? We are now looking for (Senior) Quantitative Risk Analyst to develop LGD models in Nordea. This opportunity is an exciting mix of challenges such as developing statistical models, designing internal software libraries, working with big data, analysing economic behaviour, understanding and abiding to regulatory constraints.
   

  

**About Our Team**
 Meet the LGD Models team in Risk Models Methodology & IRB Models. Our team is a part of one of the most important programs, which consists in upscaling Nordea’s internal models for the credit risk. The primary focus of this role is to develop LGD models, but there would be opportunities to work with other IRB parameters. As (Senior) Quantitative Risk Analyst you will develop your skills within advanced modelling – a field which is in high focus in Nordea.
   

  

 Being a part of the broader Group Risk organization, you'll help ensure that compliance underpins every decision we make and every action we take. By bringing your perspectives to the team and through your focus on quality and cooperation, you will be important.
   

  

 Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the values that guide us in how we work and how we make decisions – and that we imagine you share with us.
   

  

**What You’ll Be Doing**
**Main responsibilities in this role:**
* Develop, test and maintain LGD models
* Develop tools for data extraction for model development and other analysis
* Continuously work on improving methodological choices for the models and be able to clearly communicate its rationale and impact to internal and external stakeholders
* Join an open and inspiring atmosphere, where you will cooperate closely with your colleagues across the bank
* Give input to senior management and business based on data analysis and previous experience


 You will join a highly professional and dedicated team of analysts with diverse background and with excellent opportunities for personal and professional development.
   

  

**Who You Are**
**This is the right role for you if you:**
* Enjoy learning and are excited about bringing your ideas to the table;
* Take the ownership of the task, but enjoying collaboration with others;
* Have good communication skills to manage stakeholder interaction efficiently across the value chain.


**Your background and skills include:**
* Advanced degree in a quantitative field, including but not limited to economics, statistics, physics, mathematics, data science, or similar
* Experience in IRB modelling, especially in risk quantification
* Knowledge of SQL, Python, SAS or similar
* Understanding of statistical and machine learning methods
* Experience in working with data and models


**It would be ideal if you also have:**
* Experience in driving deliveries with the tight deadlines
* Knowledge of credit risk processes and bank products
* Understanding of technologies and IT solutions used for data processing


**What We Offer**
**People** 
 come here when they want to get somewhere. For some, it’s to take their career to the next level. For others, it’s to break new ground within their area of expertise – in other words, with us, you will always move forward.
   

  

**A culture** 
 that fosters performance and growth in one of the largest Nordic banks, offering various opportunities to evolve, develop and learn from brilliant colleagues with diverse backgrounds in a vibrant working environment.
   

  

**Hybrid working model** 
 – we believe in the value of bringing people together and at the same time we embrace the freedom of flexibility.
   

  

**Diversity and inclusion** 
 are a natural part of our daily work. We know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable one. We genuinely believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us stronger together. Every day we strive to find new ways to improve diversity and inclusion within our community e.g. we have signed the European Diversity Charters in the countries where we operate to show our commitment and engage with others to continue learning and improving.
   

  

 If this sounds like you, get in touch!
   

  

**Next steps**
 Submit your application no later than 16/04/2026.
   

  

**We enable dreams and aspirations for a greater good.**
**We build relationships.** 
 We add a personal touch to everything we do – when advising our customers, collaborating with colleagues, and meeting our potential candidates.
   

  

**We learn and develop.** 
 We take pride in being experts and thinking ahead. We use our expertise to meet our customers’ needs, from the simplest to the most complex. We bring a growth mindset to our work that enables us to focus on a broader perspective in our daily challenges.
   

  

**We lead change.** 
 We are responsible and aware of the impact of our decisions, both for our customers and for our local and global communities. Mindful of our responsibility towards current and future generations, we have made sustainability an integrated part of our business strategy.
   

  

**We are Nordea.** 
 We have a 200-year history of supporting and growing the Nordic economies and our values are deeply rooted in these open, progressive and collaborative societies. As one of the biggest employers in the Nordics, Poland and Estonia, you have excellent opportunities to evolve, develop and move forward with us.
   

  

**Only for candidates in Finland:** 
 A security clearance will be performed for the person selected for this position.
   

  

**Only for candidates in Sweden:** 
 For union information, please contact Finansförbundet at finansforbundet@nordea.se or SACO at SacoNordea@nordea.com.
   

  

**Only for candidates in Poland:** 
 Please include permit for processing personal data in CV as following:
   

  

 In accordance with art. 6 (1) a and b. Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 27 April 2016 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data, and repealing Directive 95/46/EC (General Data Protection Regulation) hereinafter ‘GDPR’. I agree to have: my personal data, education and employment history proceeded for the purposes of current and future recruitment processes in Nordea Bank Abp.
   

  

 The administrator of your personal data is: Nordea Bank Abp operating in Poland through its Branch, address: Aleja Edwarda Rydza Śmiglego 20, 93-281 Łodź. Your personal data will be processed for the recruitment processes in Nordea Bank Abp. You have a right to access your personal data, right to rectify and right to delete. Disclosing the personal data in the scope specified by the provisions of Polish Labour Code from 26 June 1974 and executive acts are mandatory. Providing personal data is necessary to conduct the recruitment processes. The request for the deletion of your personal data means resignation from further participation in recruitment processes and causes the immediate removal of your application. Detailed information concerning processing of your personal data can be found at: https://www.nordea.com/en/doc/nordea-privacy-policy-for-applicants.pdf
   

  

 We reserve the right to reply only to selected applications.

title: Embedded Software Engineer
description:
**Embedded Software Engineer – Wireless Access & IoT**
**Build the Future of Wireless Access with Us**
**Location:** 
 Stockholm, Sweden
   

  

 Hybrid │ Digital & Access Solutions EMEIA
   

  

 Embedded systems shape how people move, work and feel secure — and so will you.
   

  

 As a
 **Embedded Software Engineer** 
 in our Wireless Lock product unit, you help bring
 **APERIO®**
