Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.
   

  

 Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.
   

  

 Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
   

  

 Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

title: IT-samordnare till fackförbundet SULF
description:
*Söker du en roll där du får kombinera strategiskt IT‑arbete med praktisk problemlösning i vardagen? Som IT‑samordnare hos SULF får du ett helhetsansvar för IT‑miljön och stora möjligheter att påverka arbetet framåt!* 




  





**Om tjänsten** 




 Den här tjänsten är en direktrekrytering vilket innebär att rekryteringsprocessen sker genom Bravura och du anställs direkt hos fackförbundet SULF.
 



  





**Om företaget** 




 Sveriges universitetslärare och forskare (SULF) är fackförbundet för universitetslärare, forskare och doktorander och har cirka 23 000 medlemmar. SULF är en kunskapsdriven och idéburen organisation med starkt professionsfokus och en tydlig röst inom högskolesektorn.
 



  





 SULF har ett 30-tal medarbetare fördelade på tre kontor i Stockholm, Göteborg och Malmö. Denna tjänst är placerad på SULF:s kontor i Gamla stan i Stockholm. Arbetsmiljön kännetecknas av en kultur där dialog, omtanke, professionalism, värme och samarbete är centrala. Vi erbjuder ett arbetsliv med god flexibilitet, generösa villkor och hög trivsel.
 



  





 Läs gärna mer om oss på
 
 www.sulf.se
 
 .
 



  





**Arbetsuppgifter** 




 Som IT‑samordnare hos SULF får du ett brett ansvar för att planera och utveckla SULF:s IT‑miljö. Arbetet växlar mellan strategiska initiativ och konkreta operativa insatser. Från analys, kravställning och systemutveckling till löpande intern support och teknisk koordinering.
 



  





 Du ansvarar för att leda SULF:s digitaliseringsgrupp, driva prioriterade projekt och se till att interna samt externa parter arbetar mot samma mål. Rollen innebär också tät kontakt med externa IT‑leverantörer och ansvar för licenser, dokumentation, upphandlingar och driftrelaterade frågor. En viktig del av arbetet rör även SULF:s CRM‑system. Här blir du en nyckelperson i medlemsservice, förbättringsarbete och eventuellt kommande systemskifte.
 



  





 Exempel på arbetsuppgifter:
 



  




* Leda digitaliseringsgruppen och driva utvecklingsprojekt
* Samordna och följa upp leverantörer inom IT‑drift, support, hårdvara och mjukvara
* Arbeta strategiskt och operativt med SULF:s IT‑miljö och säkerställa systemintegrationer
* Hantera licenser, avtal, uppdateringar och IT‑dokumentation



  





**Utbildning, erfarenhet och personliga egenskaper** 



* Eftergymnasial utbildning inom IT eller motsvarande arbetslivserfarenhet
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta brett inom IT
* Erfarenhet av CRM‑system
* Meriterande med arbetslivserfarenhet högskolesektorn eller annan statlig verksamhet
* Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, i tal och skrift



  





 Du trivs i en roll där du växlar mellan strategiskt arbete och operativa insatser. Du tar ansvar på ett självständigt sätt, prioriterar klokt och ser till att saker blir gjorda. Du bygger relationer lätt och anpassar din kommunikation till olika målgrupper. Allt från tekniska specialister till kollegor som behöver stöd för att förstå mer komplexa delar av IT‑miljön.
 



  





 Du är nyfiken, strukturerad och analytisk. Du gillar att skapa ordning, tydlighet och du har förmågan att göra det svåra begripligt. Du trivs med att ta initiativ, följer upp i projekt och håller ihop frågor över en längre period. Rollen kräver en prestigelös och pedagogisk approach där du samarbetar nära andra och bidrar till ett tryggt och öppet arbetsklimat
 



  





**Övrig information** 




**Start** 
 : Enligt överenskommelse
 



**Plats** 
 : Ferkens gränd 4, 111 30 Stockholm
 



**Lön** 
 : Enligt överenskommelse
 



**Facklig representant:** 
 Catrine Folcker,
 
 cfo@sulf.se
 




  





 Vi använder en kompetensbaserad metodik i alla rekryteringsprocesser för att säkerställa fördomsfria urval. Vi jobbar också med löpande urval, vilket innebär att vi tar ner annonsen när tillräckligt många kandidater har ansökt. Om du blir aktuell för tjänsten kommer vi att kontakta dig för en första telefonintervju. Oavsett om du går vidare i processen eller inte så kommer du att få återkoppling på din ansökan.
 



  





 Har du frågor? Hör gärna av dig!
 



 📧 info@bravura.se
 



 📞 010-171 47 10
 



  





 Vi rekommenderar att du skickar in din ansökan omgående då vi gör ett löpande urval.
 



  





 Välkommen med din ansökan!
 



  





*#Brillante*

title: iOS Engineer
description:
At Svea Solar, we have a clear vision – to fix the planet. As Sweden’s and one of Europe’s leading players in energy technology, we are driving the energy transition with solar power and smart energy solutions. ⚡
   

  

 We’re now expanding our app team with an
 **iOS Engineer** 
 . Are you ready to join the Power Shift?
   

  

**About The Role & The Team**
 As our iOS Engineer, you’ll help shape our new app and its extensions, including the Apple Watch app. The app already supports tens of thousands (soon hundreds of thousands 😉) customers in optimizing their energy use. Our iOS app is built with SwiftUI and Swift concurrency, connected to a newly developed backend, using Apple’s latest frameworks. The minimum iOS version is 16 (but is being bumped to 18 this fall).
   

  

 You’ll work closely with our iOS Staff Engineer, Product Manager, and the rest of the app team to define and deliver on our roadmap, reporting to the Engineering Manager.
   

  

*We follow a collaboration-first policy, working three days a week from our Alvik office.*
**Your Key Responsibilities**
* Take part in defining the architecture, platform, and best practices for the development of our apps on iOS and watchOS
* Write, review, and test code in a collaborative environment with other software engineers
* Work closely with other engineers, designers, and product owners to explore new solutions to problems
* Contribute to the development roadmap on how to best further us on our way to rid the planet of fossil fuels


 Are we a match?
   

  

**We’re Looking For You Who…**
* Values team success over personal success.
* Are fluent in English - our organizational language.
* Have extensive experience in Swift, with solid knowledge of Swift concurrency and SwiftUI.
* Write readable, idiomatic, and maintainable Swift, and are happy to follow established coding guidelines and workflows.
* Have released at least one app on the App Store, or you have a project in the works you can show us.


**Bonus Points If You**
* Stays up to date with advancements in Swift 6.


 🌍 Life as a Power Shifter - What do we offer you
   

  

**The opportunity to make an impact** 
 – your work directly contributes to fixing the planet and driving the energy transition.
   

  

**About**
**A value-driven workplace** 
 – read more about our values here.
   

  

**The chance to own your results** 
 – grow, take responsibility, and power up as both we and the energy industry evolve.
   

  

**Diversity and inclusivity** 
 – different perspectives drive innovation. Here, you can be yourself and contribute with your experiences, identities, and ideas.
   

  

**Employment benefits** 
 – occupational pension, 30 days of vacation, wellness allowance, parking, and more.
   

  

**Flexibility/ hybrid work** 
 – work up to two days a week from home.
   

  

 Want to see what life at Svea Solar really looks like? Follow us on Instagram: @LifeAtSveaSolar
   

  

 ⚡Who are we
   

  

 As Sweden’s - and one of Europe’s - leading providers of energy tech and solar, we help our customers optimize their energy use with smart, connected solutions. From solar panels to heat pumps, batteries, EV chargers, and software, we’re shaping the future of energy. We call it
 **The Power Shift** 
 . And the ones driving it?
 **Power Shifters** 
 .
   

  

  

  

**About Our Recruitment Process**
* With structured interviews and assessments through Alva Labs, we make sure our recruitment process is fair, inclusive, and unbiased - because diversity is key to innovation!
* Read more about our Recruitment process here.


 👉
 **Ready to become a Power Shifter?** 
 We review applications on a rolling basis, so don’t wait to send yours in!

title: Senior ML Engineer
description:
**Description**
 We are a well-funded, early-stage startup on a mission to guide the world's money. Machine learning sits at the core of our product — powering the cash flow forecasting engine that our customers rely on every day. To accelerate this, we are hiring a
 **Senior ML Engineer** 
 to own and evolve our forecasting platform from day one.
   

  

 Are you passionate about building ML systems that operate at production scale? Do you thrive in high-growth environments where you can shape both the models and the infrastructure around them? Do you want to define how machine learning transforms cash flow forecasting for modern treasury teams? Then we'd love to hear from you.
   

  

**About Palm**
 At Palm, data is at the foundation of everything we do. We're building a sophisticated application that helps enterprises make smarter financial decisions. You will get the opportunity to own the ML engine at the heart of our product — a per-customer forecasting system built on AutoGluon, with foundation model finetuning, custom evaluation metrics, and anomaly detection. This role is your chance to take ownership of a mature, production ML system and push it to the next level as we scale from being a challenger in the market to a leader in the enterprise treasury space.
   

  

 This role isn't about building models from scratch in a notebook — it's about deeply understanding a sophisticated production system, improving it, and ensuring forecast quality for every customer. You'll work directly with our leadership team to define our ML strategy, push model accuracy, and build the infrastructure that keeps our forecasting engine reliable.
   

  

 We are defined by our values:
   

  

* Speak with Courage: We make pragmatic technical decisions and own the outcomes.
* Lead with Curiosity: We explore new ML techniques and technologies to stay ahead.
* Work with Heart: We embrace the complexity of financial data and turn it into clarity.


 You'll be working side by side with product, AI engineers, and customer operations, with a clear path to technical leadership as we scale.
   

  

**How will you make an impact?**
* Own and evolve our forecasting pipeline built on AutoGluon, including per-customer model training, ensemble selection, and prediction generation.
* Push forecast accuracy through feature engineering — designing and refining covariates for calendar patterns, cross-series signals, payment cycles, and balance anchors.
* Be at the forefront of using AI-assisted workflows.
* Maintain and improve foundation model finetuning for per-customer model adaptation using PEFT and Transformers.
* Build and improve anomaly detection and changepoint detection systems to catch data quality issues before they affect forecasts.
* Design and run offline experiments to validate model changes before they reach production, using our custom evaluation suite (MASE, DTW, WQL, and more).
* Manage our MLflow model registry for per-customer model versioning, artifact storage, and experiment tracking.
* Build and manage our ML infrastructure through infrastructure as code and support other engineers in their ML & data needs.
* Drive engineering excellence through version control, CI/CD, testing, and reusable frameworks.


**Where we work**
 Palm is a remote-first company, with team members across multiple locations. We value flexibility and believe the best work often happens outside the office.
   

  

 That said, this is a
 **core technical role** 
 . To build our ML platform collaboratively and support each other in a high-growth environment, we'll have regular in-person sessions with the engineering team and founders. We'll work together to determine the right cadence that supports both flexibility and collaboration.
   

  

**Our wish list**
**Must-haves**
* 5+ years in a machine learning or data science role with production ML experience.
* Proficiency in Python (pandas, numpy, data pipelines) since it powers our entire ML stack.
* Deep experience with time series forecasting — statistical methods, ensemble strategies, and modern frameworks.
* Comfortable with leveraging AI-assisted workflows and tools in the development process.
* Hands-on experience with deep learning — PyTorch, Transformers, and ideally PEFT/LoRA for foundation model adaptation.
* Strong feature engineering skills — domain-driven features and an intuition for what signals matter.
* Experience with statistical methods — anomaly detection, changepoint detection, and evaluation metrics beyond RMSE.
* Solid MLOps experience — model registries, experiment tracking, A/B evaluation, and production model monitoring.
* Knows their way around large-scale data tools — BigQuery for training data and metrics, PostgreSQL for model configs and task queues.
* Experience with cloud platforms (GCP preferred), infrastructure as code, and cloud cost optimisation.
* A startup mindset — being scrappy, product-driven and comfortable with ambiguity.
* Strong ownership mentality with the ability to work independently but also collaboratively drive projects to completion.


**Nice-to-haves**
* Domain knowledge in fintech, financial data, or banking systems (cash flow patterns, payment cycles, treasury operations).
* Experience with AutoML frameworks for time series.
* Experience with Airflow or similar orchestration tools for ML pipelines.
* Experience with dbt for data transformations.
* Experience with Go.
* Prior experience in a rapid-growth scale-up/startup environment.
* Contributions to open-source ML tools or communities.


**Benefits Of Working At Palm**
* Travel 🛫 Meet all your new colleagues at quarterly off sites - the last 3 were in Berlin, Rotterdam & Marbella.
* Coworking Membership 🏰 Work from home is our go-to - we love spending time with friends and family instead of commuting. We have WeWork or Rivvia memberships for when you want to escape into the city.
* Stock Options 📈 Own a large chunk of Palm and reap the rewards as you help us grow the company to a global player.
* Career Growth 💫 Clear path to a Staff ML Engineer or Head of ML role as we scale.
* Impact 💥 Your achievements will directly drive forecast accuracy — the core of Palm's value proposition.
* Pension 🧑‍🌾 Everything you need to not worry about life after work.
* Health Insurance 🩺 Best-in-class local insurances to help you get treatment quickly whenever you may need it.
* Wellness Stipend 💆🏾 500 EUR in annual wellness benefits - gyms, massages, you name it.
* Time Off 🏝️ 30 paid holidays for you to take time to recharge and relax.
* Homeoffice Stipend 💻 500 EUR to upgrade your home office

title: Technical Product Owner iBOS Data & EMS
description:
Are you passionate about building impactful SaaS products and driving technical innovation? Join us at Nordomatic Property Technologies as a Technical Product Owner for iBOS Data, where you'll lead the evolution of one of our key digital offerings in smart building connectivity.
   

  

 Nordomatic is a global leader in Smart Buildings. By combining our vast experience in system integration with visionary and innovative software solutions, we are pioneers in digitalizing building management. We aim to serve our customers, people, and planet better than yesterday. We reach our goals together by enabling a strong culture, developing people and skills, and in all that we do, we believe that the more we change today, the more we can impact tomorrow.
   

  

 Today, we have over 1,000 specialists across 33 offices in 9 countries. We are proud to be one of the world’s leading independent BMS providers.
   

  

 Cloud and SaaS offer numerous opportunities for property owners to reduce energy consumption, enhance indoor air quality, and access essential data and analytics. Nordomatic Property Tech is a lean and agile software company with large ambitions! We deliver cloud solutions for properties around the globe, combining hardware and software to help companies manage their BMS and Energy in the cloud.
   

  

 Join us on our mission to create an open and sustainable future!
   

  

**About The Job**
 Location: Stockholm, Sweden, but open to options depending on the candidate.
   

  

 Team: NPT Product Team
   

  

 Data and AI are rapidly transforming building management. From predictive maintenance and fault detection to optimizing and automating, data is the enabler that helps operate the building and analyze results.
   

  

 iBOS Data & EMS provides a simple way to securely and reliably access operational data generated by buildings, formatted for immediate use in intuitive dashboards, reports, and visualizations, as well as existing Business Intelligence (BI) or analytics platforms. Built on the iBOS stack and powered by the secure iBOS Connect technology, it enables facility managers and property teams to easily integrate building data into their preferred digital environment and explore, monitor, and act on it.
   

  

 The possibilities are endless, and we have only touched on the first small implementations.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Technical Product Owner for iBOS Data & EMS, you'll own the product roadmap and backlog for our data and energy optimizing solutions. You'll play a pivotal role in shaping products that are quickly becoming central to our customers' energy, data management, and analytics strategies. Working closely with engineering, design, and stakeholders, you'll ensure we deliver measurable value through dashboards, APIs, and scalable data solutions.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Help define and drive the vision, strategy, and roadmap for iBOS Data, aligned with business and customer goals.
* Own and continuously refine the product backlog, translating strategic objectives into clear user stories and system specifications.
* Collaborate with development teams to ensure technical feasibility, data integrity, and high-quality delivery.
* Prioritize features based on customer value, ROI, API usability, and strategic fit.
* Work closely with customers and support teams to understand data needs, pain points, and integration scenarios.
* Facilitate sprint planning, backlog grooming, and delivery ceremonies in an agile environment.
* Support QA efforts and contribute to documentation, training, and onboarding materials.
* Track dependencies, manage risks, and ensure smooth epic delivery across teams.
* Champion a data-driven culture using analytics to guide decisions and validate hypotheses.


 What You Bring
   

  

* Proven experience in technical product management, ideally within SaaS, data analytics, Energy Management Systems (EMS), or IoT domains.
* Strong understanding of data pipelines, APIs, dashboards, and BI integrations.
* Experience working with agile development teams and managing end-to-end product delivery cycles.
* Analytical mindset with the ability to make data-informed decisions and measure feature impact.
* Excellent communication skills, being able to bridge technical teams, business stakeholders, and customers.
* Familiarity with tools like Jira, Confluence, product analytics platforms, and API testing tools.
* Experience with both monolithic and microservice-based architectures.
* Comfortable facilitating design sprints, prototyping, and rapid validation of new ideas.
* Experience running A/B tests and integrating customer feedback into iterative development.


 Bonus Points
   

  

* Background in smart buildings, building automation, or energy management systems (EMS).
* Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, Grafana) or embedded analytics.
* Understanding of BACnet, Modbus, or other building data protocols.
* Passion for sustainability, digital transformation, and unlocking value from building data.
* Technical background in software engineering, data engineering, or systems architecture.


 Why Nordomatic?
   

  

 At Nordomatic, we are at a pivotal stage in our journey to become a digital and data-driven company. Our commitment to innovation enables us to transform the impossible into reality by building partnerships and ecosystems that foster scale and speed for rapid growth. With strong investment support, we aim to disrupt the industry while making a meaningful difference through our work. Here, you'll find numerous opportunities to learn, grow, and contribute to developing digital products that support sustainability every day.

title: Marketing Executive
description:
**Join our team as a Marketing Executive** 




  





 Most Studios is growing, and we need someone to help the world know it. We're a Stockholm based design agency — our clients range from ambitious startups like Legora and Tandem to global names like IKEA and Klarna.
 



  





 As our marketing lead, you'll own everything that puts us in front of the right people — content, social, events, campaigns, and collabs. You'll work directly with our founder, move fast, and have the creative freedom to build something you're genuinely proud of. If you have taste, ideas, and the drive to see them through — this one's for you.
 



  





**What we're looking for:** 



* 1–3 years of experience in marketing, ideally at an agency or fast-moving startup
* A strong creative instinct — you generate ideas, not just execute them
* Confident writer in English; Swedish is a plus
* Comfortable with GA4, Google Ads, and LinkedIn — not expert level, but not starting from zero
* A self-starter who takes a brief and runs with it
* Genuine appreciation for design and an understanding of why it matters to a business



  





**Responsibilities:** 



* Create — develop content across social, our website, and partnerships
* Activate — plan and execute events and brand collaborations that generate brand awareness and content
* Grow — set up, run, and optimize Google Ads and LinkedIn campaigns
* Measure — track performance across channels and report back with clear takeaways



  





**What you can expect from us:** 



* Fantastic office at Skeppsbron 16
* Competitive salary
* 4.5% pension on top of your monthly salary
* 3,000 SEK per year for health-related expenses
* 5 weeks of yearly vacation
* Fully equipped MacBook Pro and iPhone, with the phone bill covered
* An exceptional parental leave policy (90% of salary + 3,000 SEK per month)
* A genuine commitment to work/life balance
* Growth opportunities in a scaling business — we prioritise internal promotion
* A dynamic and supportive culture that fosters entrepreneurship and collaboration



  





 Our team consists of designers, strategists, and digital specialists who have shaped the identities and digital presence of some of the most ambitious companies in the Nordics and beyond. We partner with startups, scale-ups, and industry giants at defining moments in their growth.
 



  





 If you're ready to make your mark, send a short intro and some work samples — a campaign, a post, anything that shows how you think and create — to talent@moststudios.com

title: Partner Manager
description:
**About Froda** 




 Founded in 2015, Froda's mission is to democratize access to financing - giving small businesses the same growth opportunities as large ones. We've transformed the loan process from months to minutes, and through embedded lending, we're now integrating our technology with banks and fintechs across Europe to build the next generation of lending infrastructure.
 



  





**About the role** 




 As Froda scales its embedded lending business across Europe, we're looking for a Partner Manager to own and grow a portfolio of strategic partners, neobanks, fintechs, and financial platforms. This isn't a sales role or a support role. You'll be the person who drives performance across live integrations, leads joint planning with sophisticated counterparts, and navigates both internal and external stakeholders to get things done. One day, you're digging into a Jira ticket with the tech team, the next you're presenting a business review to a bank. We're looking for someone that is comfortable with both. You'll work cross-functionally with product, tech, credit, ops, and compliance to deliver on partner commitments, and you'll represent partner needs internally with clarity and conviction
 **.** 




  





**As Partner Manager, your responsibilities include (but are not limited to)** 



* Own a portfolio of embedded partners, with full responsibility for relationship health, performance, and growth
* Define and execute tailored plans per partner: market expansion, deeper product adoption, and new commercial opportunities
* Lead go-live and integration projects, ensuring partners are set up for long-term success
* Run regular business reviews - turning data into clear, actionable initiatives for both sides
* Push initiatives forward cross-functionally, aligning internal teams around partner priorities
* Monitor KPIs, flag risks early, and report outcomes to leadership
* Help build scalable processes as the partner function grows



  





**What we're looking for** 




 You've worked in a B2B fintech, payments, or SaaS environment, and crucially, in a role that goes beyond sales or support. You've managed the full lifecycle of a partner relationship, including technical integrations. You understand how API-based products work and can hold a credible conversation with both a bank's commercial team and their engineers. You're diplomatically sharp, able to push hard on delivery without damaging the relationship. You're energised by complexity rather than drained by it, and you move forward even when things aren't fully defined.
 



  





**You have** 



* 3+ years in partner management, account management, or business development in fintech, SaaS, or financial services
* Proven ownership of strategic B2B relationships involving technical integrations or embedded products
* Experience leading go-live or integration projects. Fintech and banking context is a strong plus
* Comfort working with partner metrics (activation, conversion, penetration) and using them to drive decisions, not just report them
* Strong stakeholder management, both internally and with sophisticated external partners
* Native-level fluency in one or more Nordic languages, plus strong written and spoken English



  





**Meriting:** 



* Experience with embedded finance or platform partnerships
* Hands-on experience with API-based products and technical partner teams
* Familiarity with tools like Tableau or SQL
* Additional European languages, particularly German, Italian, or French



  





**What’s in it for you?** 



* High ownership from day one. You'll help shape how partner management works as we scale
* A seat at the table: partners are a strategic priority, and you'll work closely with product, tech, and leadership
* A team that moves fast, cares about quality, and takes work-life balance seriously
* One of Europe's most interesting fintech scaleups, at an inflection point

title: iOS Engineer
description:
At Svea Solar, we have a clear vision – to fix the planet. As Sweden’s and one of Europe’s leading players in energy technology, we are driving the energy transition with solar power and smart energy solutions. ⚡

 . Are you ready to join the Power Shift?

 Are we a match?
   

  

**We’re Looking For You Who…**
* Values team success over personal success.
* Are fluent in English - our organizational language.
* Have extensive experience in Swift, with solid knowledge of Swift concurrency and SwiftUI.
* Write readable, idiomatic, and maintainable Swift, and are happy to follow established coding guidelines and workflows.
* Have released at least one app on the App Store, or you have a project in the works you can show us.


**Bonus points if you:**
* Stays up to date with advancements in Swift 6.


 🌍 Life as a Power Shifter - What do we offer you
   

  

**The opportunity to make an impact** 
 – your work directly contributes to fixing the planet and driving the energy transition.
   

  

**About**
**A value-driven workplace** 
 – read more about our values here.
   

  

**The chance to own your results** 
 – grow, take responsibility, and power up as both we and the energy industry evolve.
   

  

**Diversity and inclusivity** 
 – different perspectives drive innovation. Here, you can be yourself and contribute with your experiences, identities, and ideas.
   

  

**Employment benefits** 
 – occupational pension, 30 days of vacation, wellness allowance, parking, and more.
   

  

**Flexibility/ hybrid work** 
 – work up to two days a week from home.
   

  

 Want to see what life at Svea Solar really looks like? Follow us on Instagram: @LifeAtSveaSolar
   

  

 ⚡Who are we
   

  

 As Sweden’s - and one of Europe’s - leading providers of energy tech and solar, we help our customers optimize their energy use with smart, connected solutions. From solar panels to heat pumps, batteries, EV chargers, and software, we’re shaping the future of energy. We call it
 **The Power Shift** 
 . And the ones driving it?
 **Power Shifters** 
 .
   

  

  

  

**About Our Recruitment Process**
* With structured interviews and assessments through Alva Labs, we make sure our recruitment process is fair, inclusive, and unbiased - because diversity is key to innovation!
* Read more about our Recruitment process here.


 👉
 **Ready to become a Power Shifter?** 
 We review applications on a rolling basis, so don’t wait to send yours in!

title: Head of School, Materials Science and Engineering
description:
This Job is based in Australia
   

  

* One of Australia’s leading research & teaching universities
* Vibrant campus life with a strong sense of community & inclusion
* Enjoy a career that makes a difference by collaborating & learning from the best.


**At UNSW, we pride ourselves on being a workplace where the best people come to do their best work.**
**THIS ROLE IS LOCATED IN SYDNEY, AUSTRALIA.**
 The Faculty of Science is a large and one of the most complex faculties of UNSW Sydney. It comprises the Schools of Aviation, Biological Earth & Environmental Science (BEES), Biotechnology & Biomolecular Sciences (BABS), Chemistry, Materials Science & Engineering (MSE), Mathematics & Statistics, Physics and Psychology. It also includes a number of major Research Centres and field stations.
   

  

 The School of Materials Science & Engineering comprises of approximately 30 continuing academic and professional staff, and approximately 45 contractual academic and professional staff. Materials Science and Engineering is currently placed #1 in Australia in the 2025 QS World University Subject rankings. The school’s research groups are highly interconnected, with frequent interactions with other schools and faculties both inside and outside UNSW.
   

  

 The Head of School role is the key integration point between the UNSW 2025 Strategy’s strategic priorities of academic excellence, social & industry engagement and global impact, and the delivery of those priorities through the work of our staff and students and engagement with key external stakeholders. Primarily, this is achieved through integrated strategic planning, fostering an environment in which innovation and creativity can thrive, people-centred leadership and sound management of resources.
   

  

**About The Role**
* Senior Level Appointment for the term plus 17% Superannuation and annual leave loading
* Includes an underlying continuing Academic position at Level D/E
* Fixed Term – 5 years
* Full-time (35 hours per week)


 The Head of School reports directly to the Faculty Dean to shape and realise the strategic priorities and performance of the university. The academic staff of the School report to the Head of School who is supported by the School leadership team which includes a School Manager and a Deputy Head of School. The School Manager is responsible for the administrative and operational management of the School in collaboration with the Head of School and Faculty Executive Director.
   

  

**Strategy**
**Specific responsibilities for this role include:**
* Developing a strategic vision for the School that is aligned to both the Faculty strategic plan, with the view of strengthening the School’s (leading) position in Australia and all over the world.
* Contribute to the formulation of the Faculty strategic plan by providing strategic advice on external engagement, research and educational priorities.
* Lead and influence industry partners and research end-users to facilitate the development of sustainable industry-supported collaborations that lead to innovative technological outcomes


**Academic Excellence** 
  

  

* Drive the overall academic performance of the School in meeting the University’s objectives, specifically through: o Providing a valuable student education experience by fostering a culture of innovation and excellence in teaching that is aligned to performance indicators.
+ Attracting high calibre students at the undergraduate and postgraduate level (including higher degree research students), and life-long learners, consistent with the University’s student and revenue targets.
+ Enhancing the research profile of the School by improving the quality and quantity of research output including the proportion of research active staff, the quantity and size of research grants, the number and quality of publications and appropriate measures of impact such as citations.
+ Developing financially beneficial new programs and courses in collaboration with academic staff and the Faculty in order to continually meet stakeholder requirements for global graduates.
+ Identifying new prospects to collaborate with education, research and industry partners, and pursue opportunities for knowledge exchange and technology transfer where possible.
+ Growing the international presence and reputation of the School.


**Leadership**
* Build a strategic vision for the School and engage staff within the School to realise that strategic vision.
* Engage School staff to establish and maintain a collegial, supportive and productive culture within the School, and externally with Faculty and University colleagues.
* Attract and retain staff of the highest quality to enable the strategic vision of the School to be achieved.
* Build the capability of staff within the School through effective talent management and succession planning strategies supported by regular communication, quality performance conversations, coaching and addressing barriers to high performance.
* Engage in activities that build and sustain excellent relationships with external stakeholders including potential students and high schools, potential employers of our graduates, donors, alumni, the community, business, and government to ensure the interests of the School are effectively promoted and advanced.
* Pursue and support equity, diversity and inclusion objectives and policies.
* Lead the School by setting clear expectations and goals, establishing KPIs, giving regular feedback on progress and facilitating effective career development conversations.
* Be an active member of the Faculty Executive and contribute to the overall leadership, including promoting and representing MSE and Science both internally and externally.


**School Management**
* Take responsibility for devolved School budgets and comply with University financial requirements.
* Influence income and expenditure to enhance financial sustainability.
* Ensure that the School meets agreed key performance measures including those related to education, research, global impact and social engagement.
* Ensure that the School is effectively administered including optimising the allocation of resources, operating within budget and meeting relevant legislative and compliance obligations.
* Lead School based committees and boards and contribute to the Faculty and University committees as required.
* Oversee compliance and quality assurance management consistent with requirements under the University’s Risk Management Framework, including Work Health and Safety.
* Align with and actively demonstrate the Code of Conduct and Values.
* Ensure hazards and risks are identified and controlled for tasks, projects and activities that pose a health and safety risk within your area of responsibility.


 The Head of School and the Faculty Dean will agree on specific performance targets annually as part of the UNSW Senior Staff Performance Review, including targets relating to leadership responsibilities and expectations in relation to the Head of School’s own research and teaching (if applicable).
   

  

**About The Successful Applicant**
 (Selection Criteria)
   

  

**To Be Successful In This Role You Will Have**
* A respected scholar, normally at a Professorial level, in an appropriate discipline with an understanding and appreciation of the values and roles of a major university operating in an international context.
* The energy and resilience needed to deal with the challenges and opportunities offered by an ambitious university.
* A breadth of academic vision and intellectual enthusiasm that encompasses the range of disciplines within the School and understands the key relationships outside of the School.
* Capacity to articulate a strategic vision for the School and the ability to lead and manage change to achieve that vision.
* A strong, proactive and energetic leader who can motivate high performance from diverse groups of people across the School.
* Outstanding communication and interpersonal skills including the ability to encourage staff to achieve their best and to have difficult conversations with under-performing staff where necessary.
* The representational skills to effectively take the School forward in a variety of internal and external forums.
* The ability to manage budgets effectively and according to School and Faculty strategic priorities.
* A strong sense of equity, fairness and ethics to ensure a safe, diverse and respectful working environment which supports high performance and the development of staff.
* A capacity to build and maintain effective collaborative partnerships within and external to the School to support the mission and objectives of the School, Faculty and of the University
* An understanding and commitment to UNSW’s aims, objectives and values in action, together with relevant policies and guidelines.
* Ability and capacity to implement required UNSW health and safety policies and procedures.


**To apply for the position of Head of School – Materials Science & Engineering, please submit the following:**
* A cover letter
* A document that systematically addresses the selection criteria listed above.
* A statement of your leadership philosophy and vision for the School (up to 2 pages)


 For informal queries, please see the below contact details.
   

  

 Otherwise, please apply online -
 **applications will not be accepted if sent directly to the contact listed.**
**Contact** 
 :
   

  

 PJ. Sullivan
   

  

**E:** 
 pj.sullivan@unsw.edu.au
   

  

**Applications close:** 
 April 15th, 2026
   

  

**Find Out More About Working At UNSW At Www.unsw.edu.au**
*UNSW is committed to equity diversity and inclusion. Applications from women, people of culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, those living with disabilities, members of the LGBTIQ+ community; and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent, are encouraged. UNSW provides workplace adjustments for people with disability, and access to flexible work options for eligible staff. The University reserves the right not to proceed with any appointment.*

title: Upphandlare med specialistinriktning mot IT
company: Swedish Civil Defence and Resilience Agency
description:
Myndigheten för civilt försvar
   

  

*Myndigheten för civilt försvar är den ledande, inriktande och samordnande myndigheten i det civila försvaret. Vi säkerställer att samhället är förberett för kris, höjd beredskap och krig.*
 Vill du göra skillnad och tillsammans med oss arbeta för ett säkrare samhälle? Då kanske du är vår nästa kollega!
   

  

 Upphandlingsenheten expanderar och vi söker nu en upphandlare med specialistinriktning mot IT. En roll för dig som vill arbeta med kvalificerade och samhällsviktiga IT-affärer i en komplex och utvecklande miljö där kraven på säkerhet, robusthet och kvalitet är höga.
   

  

 Ditt ansvar och dina arbetsuppgifter
   

  

 Som upphandlare hos oss ansvarar du för att planera, leda och genomföra kvalificerade IT-upphandlingar inom exempelvis system, IT-infrastruktur, kommunikation och IT-tjänster.
   

  

 Du driver hela upphandlingsprocessen – från behovsanalys till avtalsuppföljning. Arbetet sker ofta i nära samarbete med verksamhetens IT-specialister och andra nyckelfunktioner inom myndigheten.
   

  

 Du arbetar självständigt i ett nära samarbete med chefer och medarbetare inom hela myndigheten. I rollen ingår även att driva förbättringsarbeten och utvecklingsfrågor inom ansvarsområdet samt bidra till enhetens gemensamma utveckling.
   

  

 Tillsammans med dina kollegor arbetar du med att utveckla, effektivisera och kvalitetssäkra myndighetens inköpsprocess. Du ger även stöd och rådgivning till verksamheten i upphandlingsfrågor.
   

  

 Dina kunskaper och kompetenser
   

  

**Vi Söker Dig Som Har**
* akademisk utbildning inom teknik/IT, juridik, ekonomi eller annat relevant område eller YH/KY-utbildning inom offentlig upphandling eller annan utbildning eller erfarenhet som vi bedömer likvärdig
* minst fem års praktisk erfarenhet inom offentlig upphandling/inköp eller annan erfarenhet inom inköp som vi bedömer likvärdig
* flerårig erfarenhet av att självständigt driva IT-upphandlingar
* god förståelse för IT-lösningar, exempelvis system, drift, infrastruktur, kommunikation, licensmodeller och säkerhetslösningar
* erfarenhet av att arbeta nära IT-specialister och översätta tekniska behov till upphandlingskrav
* goda kunskaper om lagen om offentlig upphandling (LOU)
* mycket god förmåga att uttrycka dig i tal och skrift på svenska och engelska


**Det Är Meriterande Om Du Har Erfarenhet Av**
* lagen om upphandling på försvars- och säkerhetsområdet (LUFS)
* tekniskt avancerad kravställning
* leverantörsstyrning och förhandling inom IT-avtal
* olika typer av upphandlingsförfaranden
* att arbeta med informationssäkerhet i upphandling
* avtalsuppföljning
* att genomföra upphandlingar på engelska


 I allt vårt arbete, både internt och externt, ska vi agera utifrån den statliga värdegrunden. Som alla medarbetare och ledare hos oss förväntas du agera utifrån myndighetens förhållningssätt.
   

  

 Förhållningssätten gäller i vardag, kris och krig. De ska ses som en helhet och bygger på varandra; samarbetsinriktad – proaktiv - handlingskraftig.
   

  

 Läs mer om våra förhållningssätt
   

  

 För att lyckas i rollen ser vi att du är analytisk och har förmåga att snabbt skapa dig en helhetsbild i komplexa sammanhang. Du är drivande, strukturerad och levererar med hög kvalitet.
   

  

 Du har lätt för att samarbeta, kommunicera och bygga förtroende i en miljö med många kontaktytor. Vi kommer att lägga stor vikt vid din personliga egenskaper.
   

  

 Om anställningen
   

  

 Vi erbjuder en tillsvidareanställning på heltid med inledande provanställning.
   

  

 Placeringsort är Karlstad eller Stockholm. Resor i tjänsten förekommer.
   

  

 Majoriteten av våra anställningar innebär placering i säkerhetsklass. Vanligtvis krävs svenskt medborgarskap. Vid placering i säkerhetsklass kommer säkerhetsprövning med registerkontroll att genomföras före anställning enligt 3 kap i säkerhetsskyddslagen. De flesta medarbetare på Myndigheten för civilt försvar är krigsplacerade.
   

  

 Om oss
   

  

 Upphandlingsenheten är en central funktion på myndigheten som både stödjer och styr hela myndigheten inom inköpsprocessen. Enhetens uppgift är att styra, utveckla, förvalta och följa upp myndighetens arbete med upphandling och avtalsförvaltning. Vidare ingår att inrikta och driva myndighetens upphandlingsarbete och utveckla och förvalta tillhörande systemstöd, processer och rutiner samt metoder för uppföljning av avtal och leverantörer. Upphandlingsenheten är uppdelad i team. Enheten leds av myndighetens upphandlingschef.
   

  

 Alla vi som arbetar på Myndigheten för civilt försvar förväntas och ska bidra till ökad jämställdhet och likabehandling för en arbetsmiljö och ett samhälle fritt från diskriminering.
   

  

 Läs mer om vår organisation, förmåner och hur det är att arbeta hos oss
   

  

 Vill du veta mer?
   

  

 Har du frågor är du välkommen att kontakta chef Hans Björk. Fackliga företrädare är Jörgen Mähler (Saco-S), Anna Anelius (ST) och Ann-Britt Paradis (SEKO). Samtliga nås via växelnummer 0771-240 240.
   

  

**Välkommen med din ansökan senast den 21 april 2026**
 Anställningsform Tillsvidare med provanställning Anställningens omfattning Heltid Tillträde Enligt överenskommelse Löneform Månadslön Antal lediga befattningar 1 Sysselsättningsgrad 100% Ort Karlstad eller Stockholm Län Stockholms län Land Sverige Referensnummer 2026/86 Kontakt
   

  

* Hans Björk, 0771-240240


 Publicerat 2026-03-30

title: Product & Tender Manager
description:
At Roche you can show up as yourself, embraced for the unique qualities you bring. Our culture encourages personal expression, open dialogue, and genuine connections, where you are valued, accepted and respected for who you are, allowing you to thrive both personally and professionally. This is how we aim to prevent, stop and cure diseases and ensure everyone has access to healthcare today and for generations to come. Join Roche, where every voice matters.
   

  

 The Position
   

  

 We are now looking for a
 **Product & Tender Manager** 
**within Clinical Chemistry and Immunochemistry** 
 to join our Commercial Customer Excellence Chapter at Roche Diagnostics Sweden. In this role, you will work at the intersection of product management, public procurement, and commercial strategy — contributing to sustainable business growth while supporting high-quality patient care.
   

  

 You will be part of our
 **Core Lab Customer Area team** 
 , reporting to the Core Lab Business Segment Lead. We are a collaborative and forward-looking organization where teamwork, knowledge sharing, and trust are key. You will work closely with colleagues across commercial and operational functions in Sweden, as well as with regional and global stakeholders.
   

  

 This is a Swedish-speaking position. Our office is located in Solna, Stockholm, and we are open to considering a field-based location within Sweden. Travel within Sweden (to customer sites and the Stockholm office) is a natural part of the role.
   

  

**Your Role – Making an Impact**
 As Product & Tender Manager, you will have a broad and meaningful responsibility. You will ensure that our tender processes are professional, compliant, and competitive, while also supporting the lifecycle and performance of our product portfolio.
   

  

 You will:
   

  

**Drive Successful Tender Processes**
* Monitor upcoming RFIs and public tenders
* Lead and coordinate the full tender process in close collaboration with cross-functional colleagues
* Ensure that our proposals are high quality, compliant, strategically aligned, and delivered on time
* Act as the main contact towards customers during the tender phase
* Continuously develop our value proposition based on market insights, sustainability requirements, and regulatory developments such as LOU


**Strengthen and Support Our Product Portfolio**
* Take ownership of lifecycle management for launched products within Core Lab, also supporting POC portfolio
* Ensure transparent communication regarding product updates or supply matters
* Provide structured market feedback and improvement suggestions to regional and global product teams
* Stay updated on industry trends and competitor activities to support strong business cases and proposals


 You will not do this alone. You will be supported by experienced colleagues and work in close collaboration across functions — your strength will be in bringing people together and moving processes forward.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 We are looking for someone with a life science background who enjoys responsibility and thrives in a collaborative setting. You are structured and analytical, but also pragmatic and solution-oriented. You feel comfortable coordinating complex processes and engaging different stakeholders.
   

  

 You likely bring:
   

  

* A university degree in a relevant life science field (biomedicine, biotechnology, medicine or similar)
* Experience working with healthcare products and/or disease management solutions. Preferably within Clinical Chemistry and Immunochemistry
* Experience within Point of Care diagnostics is highly meritorious, as it complements our Core Lab portfolio and supports a holistic approach to laboratory and decentralized testing solutions
* A solid understanding of the Swedish healthcare system
* Project management experience and the ability to drive processes from start to finish
* Fluency in Swedish and English, with strong written and verbal communication skills
* Experience with tenders and commercial proposal work (meritorious)
* Familiarity with CRM systems, SAP, or business data tools (a plus)


 Most importantly, you are curious, collaborative, and motivated by contributing to a healthier Sweden - where all knowledge is put to use.
   

  

 At Roche Diagnostics Sweden, you will have the opportunity to combine scientific knowledge, commercial thinking, and meaningful customer interaction — in a role where your work truly matters. We look forward to hearing from you.
   

  

 Who we are
   

  

 A healthier future drives us to innovate. Together, more than 100’000 employees across the globe are dedicated to advance science, ensuring everyone has access to healthcare today and for generations to come. Our efforts result in more than 26 million people treated with our medicines and over 30 billion tests conducted using our Diagnostics products. We empower each other to explore new possibilities, foster creativity, and keep our ambitions high, so we can deliver life-changing healthcare solutions that make a global impact.
   

  

 Let’s build a healthier future, together.
   

  

**Roche is an Equal Opportunity Employer.**

title: Game Animator
description:
**Home of the classics. Shaping the future of slots.**
 NetEnt is a leading provider of premium online casino content, delivering unforgettable gaming experiences for players across the globe. With a portfolio of over 250 game titles, we’ve been at the forefront of innovation since 1996, developing iconic slots such as Starburst, Gonzo’s Quest and Dead or Alive.
   

  

 Our mission is simple: to give operators the tools to captivate their players and keep entertainment at the heart of online gaming.
   

  

 With a strong focus on quality, fairness, and responsible gaming, NetEnt remains a trusted partner for the world’s top operators.
   

  

 NetEnt’s journey began with titles that redefined online slots. The cosmic brilliance of
 **Starburst** 
 has made it one of the most-played games in the world. Our loveable characters from
 **Gonzo’s Quest** 
 and
 **Finn and the Swirly Spin** 
 brought storytelling and personality to the reels in ways players had never experienced before.
   

  

**Job Description**
**NETENT** 
 is looking for an experienced all round
 **Game Animator** 
 with 2D professional skills and proactive life attitude, interested in work with designing, conceptualizing, and continuously bringing characters, effects, and environments to life in the NetEnt games.
   

  

 To apply for this role you should have an excellent knowledge of game art animation creation and traditional drawing. It's very desirable to have skills and knowledge in game development. Also Supply a recent portfolio.
   

  

 We are offering challenging projects, opportunities for self-development and professional growth, creative environment, friendly colleagues and international team!
   

  

**Your main work tasks will include:**
* Animate characters, environments, and FX in different styles.
* Bring characters, effects, and environments to life
* Work with programmers to achieve the best possible results with the technology available


**Qualifications**
**What's your background?**
* Professional experience in Spine.
* Excellent skills in 2D animation.
* Ability to produce animations in a variety of styles
* Experience in drawing and illustration
* Knowledge of Adobe Creative Suite, Spine or similar software
* Good knowledge of English, both written and spoken
* Good Communication skills
* Ability to perform multiple tasks and projects concurrently
* Creative mindset


**Bonus skills:**
* 3D animation (any 3D software)
* Special effects (After Effects or other software).


**Personality requirements:**
* Ability to perform multiple tasks and projects concurrently and to meet deadlines for each such task and project
* Ability to generate and implement ideas
* Be visible, vocal, objective and open to feedback
* Actively work with and improve the NetEnt culture and values
* Working with people of varied backgrounds
* Enhancing own skills


**Who are you?**
 The most important characteristics to succeed in this role are to be able to provide animations in a variety of styles, keep deadlines, and be self-motivated.
   

  

 You are communicative, analytical, and prone to take initiative. You are flexible, thorough and enjoy working in a fast paced, dynamic environment and work well under pressure.
   

  

 We look forward to your application in English including a letter in which you introduce yourself and tell us about your interest in the specific position.
   

  

**Additional Information**
 Exciting opportunity at our
 **NetEnt Game Studio** 
 in the heart of Gothenburg with amazing colleagues! Join our dynamic international team and be a part of a vibrant work environment where innovation thrives. Embrace the city´s energy as you contribute to our company's growth! NetEnt provides a dynamic and creative work environment with a unique opportunity for the right people to enhance their skills and drive their passion.
   

  

**Our Benefits Are**
* Competitive pension, health and medical insurance
* 30 paid vacation days
* Fitness benefit (Friskvårdsbidrag)
* Professional growth
* Sport activities, parties, team buildings


 Today, every NetEnt game is crafted to combine innovation with style. From cutting-edge mechanics to stunning visuals, our portfolio is built for the modern player while staying true to the classics that made us who we are.
   

  

 Now, all of our games can be played right here on our official site. Whether you’re exploring the next big release on our roadmap or revisiting a legend in our games section.If you want to be part of one of the most exciting industries in the world, then apply now and be a key player in our success story!
   

  

 We appreciate all applications and interest in NetEnt.
 **This is a 100% on-site position.** 
 Based in Stockholm, Sweden. This position is
 **offered as a 12-month initial contract,** 
 with a possibility of extension.

title: Head of Finance and Accounting
description:
**About the role** 




 Zendr is seeking an experienced Head of Finance and Accounting who will play a key role during the scale-up period.
 



 As the one of the leaders of the finance team, you will be focused on building and growing the finance function, implementing best practice around reporting both internally and externally for investors. You will have strong control of the financial numbers, and required to partner closely with the CFO and other heads of departments to continue to drive Zendr’s ambitious growth plans.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 




**Financial Leadership & Stewardship:** 



* Own all finance-related operations, core accounting, financial reporting, payroll and invoicing processes
* Lead and develop a team of currently two finance managers who are owning customer and supplier invoicing
* Implement robust accounting policies, compliance processes, and internal controls, for example around accruals and cut-off dates
* Effectively manage the current outsourced book-keeping agency, while progressively internalizing critical accounting activities to build a disciplined in-house finance function
* Drive automation of the finance function and use software to boost efficiency, speed, accuracy, and quality of insights wherever possible
* Lead the consolidation of accounts from different group entities as the company expands



  





**Financial Strategy & Infrastructure:** 



* Support the CFO in building financial insights and KPIs by ensuring accurate, timely, and well structured underlying financial data.
* Develop capital-efficient financing and funding strategies, including alternative sources such as debt financing, grants, and tax credits
* Support accounting related M&A activities, including financial due diligence and financial PMI
* Design and implement a scalable finance infrastructure, including systems, processes, data models as well as the use of AI that support Zendr’s continued growth



  





**Board & Shareholder Communications:** 



* Produce robust and timely monthly management accounts and KPIs for reporting to the management team and the board



  





**About you** 




 The ideal candidate will be a strategic and results-oriented financial operator with a proven track record in high-growth operationally-intensive companies, who has been responsible for financial processes and outputs and delivered tangible improvements to financial processes.
 



  





**Key attributes of the successful candidate include:** 



* **Ownership & Mindset:** 
 Comfortable being personally accountable for financial accuracy and engaging directly with key stakeholders. Brings a hands-on, can-do attitude and pragmatic problem-solving mindset, valuing ownership, curiosity, and execution as highly as prior experience.
* **Professional Training:** 
 Formal training in accounting (audit firm or equivalent), preferably with Big 4 or leading accounting firm background combined with hands-on in-house experience, ideally from a transaction-heavy or platform-based business
* **Prior Experience:** 
 At least five years of experience in accounting, financial control, managing day-to-day accounting, finance and cash management
* **Demonstrated ownership and leadership:** 
 Clear examples of leading key financial processes hands-on and delivering robust financial outputs for management review
* **Financial Expertise:** 
 Thorough understanding of metrics and financial ops, with the ability to lead all aspects of accounting, financial reporting, and compliance.
* **Financial Control Excellence:** 
 Ability to execute fast and accurate month ends, high quality reporting, including complex accruals and can deal with complexities of cross-border entities (e.g legal set up, payroll, tax structuring)
* **Operational Efficiency:** 
 Experience automating finance functions for increased efficiency, speed, and accuracy, as well as managing vendor relationships to optimise terms, service levels and expenditure.
* **Communication Skills:** 
 Proven ability to foster relationships across the organization, strong communication and presentation skills.



  





**About Zendr** 




 Zendr is a fast-growing technology company headquartered in Stockholm, founded in 2020 by co-founders Johan Sund and Mathias Rebane. Zendr offers a user-friendly, B2B transportation administration platform enabling customers to compare rates, book freight as well as manage and track shipments, targeting SMEs with complex shipping needs across all industries. The platform currently serves approximately 2,000+ businesses (sector agnostic), including prominent brands such as Halberg-Rassy, Swedish Agro, and BJÖRN BORG. The company has grown to operate in Sweden, Norway, and Denmark with an operational team of 60+ employees.
 



 The platform is an all-in-one offering with flexible freight solutions through pre-negotiated freight rates with leading carriers, regardless of shipment type or destination, combined with in-house customer service supporting complex freight queries. This enables customers to compare rates, book, track, and manage all shipments within a single, automated system, designed to reduce operational complexity and costs. It features API integrations with key ERP and e-commerce systems to help teams access logistics data and automate shipping workflows, as well as an in-house developed proprietary cloud-platform, reducing the complexity of business logistics by simplifying and automating shipments, saving time and reducing costs for customers.
 



  





 Following years of rapid, profitable growth, Zendr is entering its next phase of expansion through a new strategic partnership with a European Private Equity investor, providing significant capital and expertise to accelerate Zendr’s clear technology advantage and execute their next phase of growth.
 



 The new Head of Finance and Accounting will be a key member of the finance leadership team reporting to the CFO, Paul Kirchmeier, with end-to-end responsibility for accounting and financial operations such as day-to-day control, monthly closings, management reporting.The role will also play a central part in building a scalable finance infrastructure - spanning systems, processes and controls - to support Zendr’s expansion into a European champion.
 



  





**Our hiring process** 




 We respect your time and aim to make the recruitment journey fast, transparent and enjoyable:
 


* Application review – We’ll review your application promptly and get back to you within a few days.
* Screening interview - An informal video call to learn more about you and share who we are.
* Practical test - A practical task you'll receive in order for us to understand your expertise better.
* On-site interview - An interview where you'd come to the office and get a better understanding of the role and Zendrs journey ahead.
* Final meeting - Meet the team, talk through the role in detail, and see if we’re the right fit for each other.



 From first contact to final decision, you can expect clear communication, timely feedback, and respect for your time - because great talent deserves a great candidate experience.
 



  





**Additional information** 




 We review applications on a rolling basis, which means the position may be filled before the closing date. If you have any questions regarding the role or the recruitment process you're more then welcome to contact Ellen Plym via ellen.plym@zendr.se
 



 Start date: As soon as possible
 



 Scope: Full-time, permanent position
 



 Location: Stockholm, Solna

title: Cloud Security Specialist | Saab | Stockholm
description:
*Vill du vara med och säkra avancerade molnmiljöer i en kontext där säkerhet, tillit och kontinuerlig regelefterlevnad är avgörande? Brinner du för att bygga säkra system i stor skala - med automatisering och compliance by design som ledstjärnor? Då är det här rollen för dig!* 




  





**Din roll** 




 Som Cloud Security Specialist hos Saab spelar du en nyckelroll i att säkerställa att molnbaserade system är säkra, motståndskraftiga och löpande förenliga med interna krav och internationella säkerhetsregelverk. Du arbetar nära arkitekter, utvecklare och IT-drift för att designa, implementera och förbättra molnsäkerhetslösningar - med starkt fokus på automatisering, policy-as-code och compliance by design.
 



  





 I praktiken innebär det att du designar säkra molnarkitekturer med inbyggda kontroller, driver kontinuerlig regelefterlevnad genom automatisering och övervakning, identifierar risker och definierar säkerhetsåtgärder i miljöer som AWS eller Azure, samt utvecklar och underhåller policyer, standarder och styrmodeller för molnsäkerhet. Du integrerar säkerhetskontroller i CI/CD-pipelines, genomför riskbedömningar och sårbarhetshantering, och stödjer arbetet med IAM, kryptering, loggning, övervakning och konfigurationshantering.
 



  





 Kort sagt: du är den som ser till att molnet inte bara fungerar - utan att det är säkert, styrbart och rätt från grunden.
 



  





**Din profil** 




 Vi söker dig som har ett starkt säkerhetstänk och ett genuint intresse för att bygga säkra och regelrätta system i stor skala. Du är bekväm med att arbeta i reglerade miljöer och övertygad om att automatisering är nyckeln till att upprätthålla säkerhet och regelefterlevnad över tid. Som person är du nyfiken och driven, med starkt självledarskap och förmåga att navigera i komplexa sammanhang.
 



  





  





**För att lyckas i denna roll, bör du ha följande kvalifikationer:** 



* Relevant akademisk utbildning eller motsvarande erfarenhet inom IT-säkerhet, molnteknik eller närliggande område
* Flera års erfarenhet av molnsäkerhet i AWS- och/eller Azure-miljöer
* Praktisk erfarenhet av kontinuerlig regelefterlevnad, säkerhetsautomatisering och policy-as-code
* Kunskap om säkerhetsramverk och standarder som NIST, ISO/IEC 27001, CIS och NATO:s informationssäkerhetsstandarder
* Erfarenhet av verktyg och metoder för automatiserad regelefterlevnad, konfigurationshantering och säkerhetsövervakning
* Erfarenhet av DevSecOps och att integrera säkerhetskontroller i CI/CD-flöden
* God kommunikationsförmåga på svenska och engelska, i både tal och skrift



  





**Meriterande:** 



* Relevanta certifieringar inom molnsäkerhet (t.ex. AWS Security Specialty, Azure Security Engineer, CISSP)
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta med säkerhetskrav i försvars- eller myndighetsmiljöer
* Erfarenhet av Infrastructure as Code (t.ex. Terraform, CloudFormation)
* Intresse för zero trust-arkitektur och moderna säkerhetsprinciper



  





**Vad du blir en del av** 




**Common Tech** 
 har i uppdrag att skapa gemensamma tekniska förutsättningar för hela Saab. Genom att utveckla och förvalta gemensamma plattformar, tekniska ramverk och arbetssätt möjliggör Common Tech ett effektivt, enhetligt och långsiktigt teknikutvecklingsarbete över hela organisationen. Målet är att ge Saabs team rätt tekniska grund för att kunna utveckla avancerade produkter med hög kvalitet, säkerhet och tillförlitlighet.
 



  





**Enablement Tech** 
 är en av flera avdelningar inom Common Tech och ansvarar för att bygga, vidareutveckla och förvalta de gemensamma tekniska plattformar som möjliggör Saabs utvecklingsarbete i praktiken. Här skapas förutsättningarna för att utveckling ska kunna ske snabbt, säkert och kontrollerat - i stor skala - genom stabila plattformar, verktyg och tekniska tjänster som används brett över organisationen.
 



  





 Fokus inom Enablement Tech ligger på att möjliggöra och accelerera. Genom välarkitekturerade lösningar inom bland annat cloud, utvecklingsmiljöer, plattformsautomation och gemensam infrastruktur görs det möjligt för produkt- och utvecklingsteam att fokusera på att bygga funktionalitet och affärsvärde, snarare än att hantera grundläggande tekniska förutsättningar. Arbetet handlar om att skapa återanvändbara, skalbara och långsiktigt hållbara plattformar som andra team kan lita på och bygga vidare på.
 



  





**Saab** 
 är ett ledande försvars- och säkerhetsföretag med ett tydligt uppdrag: att bidra till att skydda människor och samhällen. Med omkring 20 000 medarbetare världen över utvecklar Saab avancerad teknik i nära samarbete mellan människor, kompetenser och discipliner. Företaget präglas av en inkluderande kultur där samarbete, lärande och långsiktig utveckling står i centrum.
 



  





*Dessa positioner kräver godkänd säkerhetsprövning enligt den svenska säkerhetsskyddslagen.* 




  





**Ansökan** 




 Den här rekryteringsprocessen hanteras av Jefferson Wells för SAABS räkning, vilket innebär att du kommer att bli anställd direkt av SAAB.
 



  





 Vi tar ej emot ansökningar via email pga GDPR.
 



  





**Urval och intervjuer sker löpande. Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!**

title: Cash Manager
description:
Dometic is a global leader in outdoor living solutions, providing essential gear for an adventurous lifestyle. We are on an exciting growth and transformation journey – now looking for our next star – a data driven and communicative Cash Manager to join the Headquarters in Solna Strand, Stockholm.
   

  

**About The Position**
**As a Cash Manager** 
 in the Treasury team, you will be involved in supporting and developing Dometic Group’s global liquidity structure and financial risk management framework. In this role, you will contribute directly to the Group’s overall financial stability by ensuring efficient cash pooling, accurate forecasting and a well‑functioning liquidity planning process. You will work in close collaboration with subsidiaries worldwide, act as a key point of expertise within Cash Management and play an integral role in strengthening the Group Treasury function through continuous improvement and digital development.
   

  

**Your Main Responsibilities**
* Prepare, analyze and develop the Group’s liquidity management and payment processes to ensure optimal cash flow and financial stability
* Deliver accurate cash forecasting to support strategic decision-making
* Oversee cash management administration across the organization
* Take full ownership of the netting process within the Group
* Co‑ordinate the Dometic Global insurance program and related administration
* Continuously provide training and guidance to subsidiaries on matters related to Cash Management in order to increase competence within the Group
* Play an active role in efficiency improvement and digitalization initiatives to modernize and streamline treasury operations
* Support in monthly reporting


**What do we offer?**
 You are offered an exciting opportunity in a dynamic, fast‑paced, and global environment where you will play a key role in Dometic’s cash and liquidity management. This is a role where your ideas matter, your impact is visible, and your interest in digitalization and smarter ways of working is encouraged. Join a company on an ambitious transformation journey, with great opportunities to grow and take on new challenges.
   

  

 ,
   

  

**A suitable background would be 6-8 years** 
 of experience within Finance, including 3–5 years of relevant Cash Management experience within corporate treasury—ideally in a multinational environment—and solid experience within corporate cash management and liquidity planning – as well as the following qualifications and experiences:
   

  

* Experience from Cash Management within banking is considered an advantage
* Strong interest in digitalization, system development and automation
* Experience in implementing or supporting efficiency‑driven digital initiatives
* Early adopter of AI with the ability to apply technology to enhance processes
* Experience from Treasury Systems is meritorious
* University bachelor’s degree in business administration or finance


**To be successful in this role, we believe that you possess the following skills, competencies & characteristics:**
* Professional, unpretentious and communicative with a collaborative approach
* Strong self‑motivation and the ability to work independently with high accountability
* A team‑oriented mindset combined with a constructive and solution‑focused attitude
* Structured, analytical and detail‑oriented working style
* Growth mindset with the ability to understand, prioritize and act on the broader financial context
* A proactive and positive “Yes I can” attitude, with the confidence to drive initiatives forward


**Dometic's Core Values**
 To thrive and succeed in this role, you understand the importance of our core values – Together We build our future, We play to win, We embrace change and We walk the talk; these values reflect the heart and soul of Dometic and they define what it takes to work here and how we do things.
   

  

 Dometic operates with a 4+1 policy, 4 days per week in the office and the possibility to work 1 day per week remotely. We see the social aspect of being in the office, meeting colleagues, having short coffee break interactions or a quick face to face meeting as key to success as we become more productive and fast paced in terms of problem solving, learning, cross functional collaboration and not the least in a way to have fun at work!
   

  

**Are you our next star?** 
 Then we would love to see your application.
   

  

 In this recruitment process Dometic is collaborating with Future Value. If you have any questions, please contact the responsible recruitment consultant, Susanne Karlsson, 070-977 27 94, susanne.karlsson@futurevalue.se
   

  

 Please submit your application through Future Value’s website via the following link: futurevalue.se/jobs/3244-cash-manager/

title: Junior Associate till Mainstream Corporate
description:
**Om oss**
 Linklaters är en internationellt ledande affärsjuridisk advokatbyrå med huvudkontor i London. Med över 5600 anställda och 31 kontor är vi etablerade i alla stora affärs- och finanscentra och kan erbjuda våra klienter förstklassig juridisk rådgivning världen över.
   

  

 Vårt Stockholmskontor fokuserar på komplex transaktionsrådgivning inom M&A, Private Capital och Banking & Finance och arbetar integrerat med Linklaters övriga kontor världen över. Hos oss utvecklar du din affärsjuridiska kompetens och bygger ett starkt lokalt och internationellt nätverk, samtidigt som du bistår våra klienter i deras mest komplexa transaktioner och ärenden. Läs mer om Linklaters på www.linklaters.com.
   

  

**Vem vi söker**
 Vi söker nu nyexaminerade jurister med start i september 2026. Du har utmärkta akademiska meriter och vill arbeta i team på en internationell byrå med tydligt transaktionsfokus. Du har mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska. Servicekänsla, noggrannhet och god analytisk förmåga är också av stor vikt. Du är en positiv och öppen person som är intresserad av att lära dig och av att bidra till både verksamhetens och din personliga utveckling.
   

  

**Vårt erbjudande**
 Du erbjuds en spännande tjänst på en internationell ledande advokatbyrå där det finns stora möjligheter till utveckling. Du får dagligen arbeta i en internationell miljö med topprankade jurister och marknadens mest komplexa ärenden, både i Stockholm och runt om i världen. Vi är en innovativ byrå och det finns möjligheter att utvecklas och engagera sig inom många olika områden såsom Legal Tech, ESG och CSR. Sedan ett år tillbaka arbetar vi i helt nya lokaler i centralt läge på Jakobsbergsgatan 24.
   

  

 Linklaters erbjuder generösa anställningsförmåner. Detta innefattar ett brett utbud av globala utbildningar, vilket ger dig ett unikt och mycket eftertraktat utbildningspaket. Genom att arbeta hos oss lär du snabbt känna kollegor från våra andra kontor runtom i världen och kan bygga upp ett internationellt nätverk som varar livet ut. Detta möjliggörs även genom några av våra olika varianter av secondment där du får arbeta på något av våra övriga kontor. Du får även tidig exponering mot klienter och ansvar att leda stora transaktioner. Detta är möjligt med vår unika plattform som kommer träna och stötta dig i din karriär.
   

  

**Ansökan**
 För att söka tjänsten skickar du in ditt CV, personliga brev och studieintyg. Blir du aktuell för tjänsten kommer vi be dig att genomföra ett engelska- samt ett kapacitetstest.
   

  

 För frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta:
   

  

 Marie Lundqvist, HR Advisor, marie.lundqvist@linklaters.com
   

  

**Technical Skills**
**This list of duties and responsibilities above is not exhaustive. It is intended to describe the general content of, and requirements for, the performance of this job. As such, the role may also include the undertaking of additional tasks as required.**

title: Product Manager - Drivers Experience
description:
**We’ve signed up to an ambitious journey. Join us!**
*As Arrive, we guide customers and communities towards brighter futures and more livable cities, it isn’t a challenge just anyone could take on. Luckily, we have something to help us make it happen. Our people and our values. We Arrive Curious, Focused and Together. Just as our entire brand is inspired by the North Star, the shining light leading travelers to their destinations since time began, our values guide us. They help us be at our best. For our customers. For the cities and communities we serve. For ourselves. As a global team, we are transforming urban mobility. Let’s grow better, together.*
**The Role**
 As a Product Manager on the Drivers Experience team, you will own
 **core account and vehicle experiences** 
 used by
 **over** 
**60 million active users annually** 
 . The
 **vehicles functionality is a prerequisite for the app’s core experiences** 
 , including parking, tolling, and other mobility features, and is relied on by millions of drivers every day. You’ll define and shape how users manage their accounts, vehicles, and preferences across the app, while driving key growth surfaces like vehicle onboarding, tolling, and parking permits.
   

  

 This role is central to the app’s
 **foundation, scale, and international expansion** 
 , giving you the opportunity to make a real impact on usability, growth, and stability across markets. If you enjoy
 **solving complex product problems, balancing user needs with regulatory and technical constraints, and building experiences that millions rely on** 
 , this role is for you.
   

  

**How To Make An Impact**
* Lead product discovery, understanding user needs and problems, and defining solutions that are valuable, usable, feasible, and viable.
* Own the full product lifecycle: from ideation, prioritization, and delivery, to tracking success and determining when to sunset features.
* Lead the adoption of AI to supercharge decision making, speed up discovery, and streamline daily workflows, ensuring the team leverages the latest technology to drive impact.
* Set, analyze and be accountable for metrics and KPIs to measure the impact of features and core product experience.
* Influence cross-functional teams and stakeholders without formal authority, ensuring alignment across engineering, design, analytics, and business partners.
* Balance growth, stability, and scalability, making trade offs informed by user insights, data, and business context.
* Advocate for the user perspective in all discussions and ensure experiences are intuitive, reliable, and delight drivers.


**About You - Your Background**
